Read a string from the first CLI arg and print whether every character is a letter.

(module alpha_check

(fn is_alpha s string -> bool
  (set n (len s))
  (if (eq n 0) (ret false))
  (set result true)
  (for i 0 n
    (set code (string_get s i))
    (set is_upper (and (ge code 65) (le code 90)))
    (set is_lower (and (ge code 97) (le code 122)))
    (if (not (or is_upper is_lower))
      (set result false)
      (break)))
  (ret result))

(fn main -> int  (if (eq (len args) 0)
    (println "Usage: program <string>")
    (ret 1))
  (set s $0)
  (if (is_alpha s)
    (println "true")
    (else
      (println "false")))
  (ret 0))
)

Read a space-separated list of words from the first CLI arg and group anagrams together.

(module anagram_finder

(fn sort_string s string -> string
  (set codes [])
  (set n (len s))
  (for i 0 n
    (set codes (push codes (string_get s i))))
  (set codes (sort codes))
  (set result "")
  (set clen (len codes))
  (for i 0 clen
    (set result (add result (char_from_code (array_get codes i)))))
  (ret result))

(fn word_key w string -> string
  (ret (sort_string (lower w))))

(fn main -> int
  (set args (argv))
  (set count (len args))
  (if (lt count 2)
    (println "Provide at least 2 words as arguments")
    (ret 1))
  (set groups {})
  (for i 0 count
    (set word (array_get args i))
    (set key (word_key word))
    (if (in key groups)
      (set grp (map_get groups key))
      (set grp (push grp word))
      (set groups (map_set groups key grp))
      (else
        (set grp [])
        (set grp (push grp word))
        (set groups (map_set groups key grp)))))
  (set found false)
  (set keys (map_keys groups))
  (set klen (len keys))
  (for i 0 klen
    (set key (array_get keys i))
    (set grp (map_get groups key))
    (if (gt (len grp) 1)
      (set found true)
      (println (fmt "Anagrams: {}" (string_join grp " ")))))
  (if (not found)
    (println "No anagrams found"))
  (ret 0))
)

Read an integer from the first CLI arg and print whether it is an Armstrong number (narcissistic).

(module armstrong
  (fn is_armstrong num int -> bool
    (set s (str num))
    (set n_digits (len s))
    (set total 0)
    (for i 0 n_digits
      (set ch (string_slice s i 1))
      (set digit (int ch))
      (set powered (floor (pow (cast_int_float digit) (cast_int_float n_digits))))
      (set total (add total powered)))
    (ret (eq total num)))

  (fn main -> int
    (set n #0)
    (for i 1 (add n 1)
      (if (is_armstrong i)
        (println i)))
    (ret 0)))

Read an integer N and print all Armstrong numbers from 1 to N.

(module armstrong_numbers

(fn count_digits num int -> int
  (set s (str num))
  (ret (len s)))

(fn int_pow base int exp int -> int
  (set result 1)
  (for i 0 exp
    (set result (mul result base)))
  (ret result))

(fn digit_power_sum num int num_digits int -> int
  (set s (str num))
  (set n (len s))
  (set total 0)
  (for i 0 n
    (set ch (string_get s i))
    (set d (sub ch 48))
    (set total (add total (int_pow d num_digits))))
  (ret total))

(fn is_armstrong num int -> bool
  (set nd (count_digits num))
  (set dps (digit_power_sum num nd))
  (ret (eq dps num)))

(fn main -> int
  (set n 10000)
  (if (gt (argv_count) 0)
    (set n #0))
  (println (fmt "Armstrong numbers up to {}:" n))
  (for i 1 (add n 1)
    (if (is_armstrong i)
      (println i)))
  (ret 0))
)

Write a Sigil program that implements array bounds demo.

(module array_bounds_demo

(fn safe_get arr array idx int -> int
  (set result -1)
  (try
    (set result (array_get arr idx))
  (catch err string
    (println (fmt "Error accessing index {}: {}" idx err))))
  (ret result))

(fn main -> int
  (set arr [10 20 30])

  (set v1 (safe_get arr 1))
  (println (fmt "arr[1] = {}" v1))

  (set v2 (safe_get arr 99))
  (println (fmt "arr[99] = {}" v2))

  (try
    (set x (array_get arr -1))
    (println x)
  (catch msg string
    (println (fmt "Caught negative index: {}" msg))))

  (try
    (set big [100 200])
    (set y (array_get big 5))
    (println y)
  (catch msg string
    (println (fmt "Caught on small array: {}" msg))))

  (ret 0))

)

Read a string and print 'yes' if brackets ()[]{} are balanced, 'no' otherwise.

(module balanced_parens

(fn is_balanced s string -> bool
  (set stack [])
  (set n (len s))
  (set valid true)
  (set i 0)
  (while (and (lt i n) valid)
    (set ch (string_get s i))
    (if (eq ch 40)
      (push stack ch))
    (if (eq ch 41)
      (if (eq (len stack) 0)
        (set valid false)
        (else
          (array_pop stack))))
    (set i (add i 1)))
  (if valid
    (ret (eq (len stack) 0)))
  (ret false))

(fn main -> int
  (set tests ["()" "(())" "(()())" "((()))" "" "(" ")" ")(" "(()" "(()))"])
  (set n (len tests))
  (for i 0 n
    (set s (array_get tests i))
    (set result (is_balanced s))
    (println (fmt "\"{}\" -> {}" s (str result))))
  (ret 0))
)

Binary-search a sorted space-separated int array (arg1) for a target (arg2). Print the 0-based index or -1.

(module binary_search

  (fn binary_search arr array target int -> int
    (set low 0)
    (set high (sub (len arr) 1))
    (while (le low high)
      (set mid (div (add low high) 2))
      (set val (int (array_get arr mid)))
      (if (eq val target)
        (ret mid)
        (else
          (if (lt val target)
            (set low (add mid 1))
            (else
              (set high (sub mid 1)))))))
    (ret -1))

  (fn main -> int
    (println "Enter sorted space-separated numbers:")
    (set line (trim (read_line)))
    (set parts (split line " "))
    (println "Enter target:")
    (set target (int (trim (read_line))))
    (set idx (binary_search parts target))
    (if (eq idx -1)
      (println "Not found")
      (else
        (println (fmt "Found at index: {}" idx))))
    (ret 0))
)

Binary-search a sorted space-separated int array (arg1) for a target (arg2). Print the 0-based index or -1.

(module binary_search_main

(fn binary_search arr array target int -> int
  (set lo 0)
  (set hi (sub (len arr) 1))
  (set result -1)
  (while (le lo hi)
    (set mid (div (add lo hi) 2))
    (set val (int (array_get arr mid)))
    (cond
      ((eq val target)
        (set result mid)
        (break))
      ((lt val target)
        (set lo (add mid 1)))
      (true
        (set hi (sub mid 1)))))
  (ret result))

(fn main -> int
  (set line (trim (read_line)))
  (set parts (split line " "))
  (set target_str (trim (read_line)))
  (set target (int target_str))
  (set idx (binary_search parts target))
  (if (eq idx -1)
    (println "Not found")
  (else
    (println (fmt "Found at index {}" idx))))
  (ret 0))
)

Read a binary string and print its decimal value.

(module binary_to_decimal

  (fn binary_to_decimal bin string -> int
    (set result 0)
    (set n (len bin))
    (for i 0 n
      (set ch (string_get bin i))
      (set result (mul result 2))
      (if (eq ch 49)
        (set result (add result 1))))
    (ret result))

  (fn main -> int    (if (lt (len args) 1)
      (println "Usage: program <binary_string>")
      (ret 1))
    (set bin $0)
    (set dec_val (binary_to_decimal bin))
    (println (fmt "Binary: {} -> Decimal: {}" bin dec_val))
    (ret 0)))

Bubble-sort space-separated integers and print them space-separated.

(module bubble_sort

(fn parse_ints strs array -> array
  (set nums [])
  (set n (len strs))
  (for i 0 n
    (set nums (push nums (int (array_get strs i)))))
  (ret nums))

(fn bubble_sort arr array -> array
  (set n (len arr))
  (for i 0 n
    (for j 0 (sub (sub n i) 1)
      (set a (array_get arr j))
      (set b (array_get arr (add j 1)))
      (if (gt a b) (set arr (array_set arr j b)) (set arr (array_set arr (add j 1) a)) (else))))
  (ret arr))

(fn ints_to_strings arr array -> array
  (set result [])
  (set n (len arr))
  (for i 0 n
    (set result (push result (str (array_get arr i)))))
  (ret result))

(fn main -> int
  (set line (read_line))
  (set parts (split line " "))
  (set nums (parse_ints parts))
  (set sorted (bubble_sort nums))
  (println (string_join (ints_to_strings sorted) " "))
  (ret 0))

)

Caesar cipher: first arg is 'encode' or 'decode', second is integer shift, third is the text; shift only ASCII letters, keep case, leave other characters unchanged.

(module caesar_cipher

(fn shift_char ch int shift int -> string
  (cond
    ((and (ge ch 65) (le ch 90))
     (ret (char_from_code (add 65 (mod (add (sub ch 65) shift) 26)))))
    ((and (ge ch 97) (le ch 122))
     (ret (char_from_code (add 97 (mod (add (sub ch 97) shift) 26)))))
    (true (ret (char_from_code ch)))))

(fn process text string shift int -> string
  (set result "")
  (set n (len text))
  (for i 0 n
    (set ch (string_get text i))
    (set result (add result (shift_char ch shift))))
  (ret result))

(fn main -> int
  (if (lt (argv_count) 3)
    (println "Usage: caesar_cipher <encode|decode> <shift> <text>")
    (ret 1))  (set mode $0)
  (set shift_str $1)
  (set text $2)
  (set raw_shift (int shift_str))
  (set norm_shift (mod raw_shift 26))
  (cond
    ((string_equals mode "encode")
     (println (process text norm_shift)))
    ((string_equals mode "decode")
     (println (process text (mod (sub 26 norm_shift) 26))))
    (true
     (println "Error: mode must be encode or decode")
     (ret 1)))
  (ret 0))
)

Title-case each word in a sentence.

(module capitalize_words

(fn capitalize_word word string -> string
  (if (eq (len word) 0)
    (ret word))
  (set first (string_slice word 0 1))
  (set rest (string_slice word 1 (sub (len word) 1)))
  (set upper_first (upper first))
  (ret (add upper_first rest)))

(fn main -> int
  (set text (stdin_read_all))
  (set trimmed (trim text))
  (set words (split trimmed " "))
  (set result [])
  (set n (len words))
  (set word "")
  (set capitalized "")
  (for i 0 n
    (set word (array_get words i))
    (set capitalized (capitalize_word word))
    (set result (push result capitalized)))
  (println (string_join result " "))
  (ret 0))
)

Write a Sigil program that implements casting demo.

(module casting_demo

(fn main -> int
  (set i 42)
  (set f 3.75)
  (set d 19.99d)

  (println "--- int to float ---")
  (set i_to_f (cast_int_float i))
  (println (fmt "{} -> {}" (str i) (str i_to_f)))

  (println "--- float to int (truncates toward zero) ---")
  (set f_to_i (cast_float_int f))
  (println (fmt "{} -> {}" (str f) (str f_to_i)))

  (set neg_f -3.75)
  (set neg_f_to_i (cast_float_int neg_f))
  (println (fmt "{} -> {}" (str neg_f) (str neg_f_to_i)))

  (println "--- int to decimal ---")
  (set i_to_d decimal (cast_int_decimal i))
  (println (fmt "{} -> {}" (str i) (str i_to_d)))

  (println "--- decimal to int (truncates) ---")
  (set d_to_i (cast_decimal_int d))
  (println (fmt "{} -> {}" (str d) (str d_to_i)))

  (println "--- float to decimal ---")
  (set f_to_d decimal (cast_float_decimal f))
  (println (fmt "{} -> {}" (str f) (str f_to_d)))

  (println "--- decimal to float ---")
  (set d_to_f (cast_decimal_float d))
  (println (fmt "{} -> {}" (str d) (str d_to_f)))

  (println "--- precision: float vs decimal for 0.1 + 0.2 ---")
  (set fa 0.1)
  (set fb 0.2)
  (set fsum (add fa fb))
  (println (fmt "float:   0.1 + 0.2 = {}" (str fsum)))

  (set da 0.1d)
  (set db 0.2d)
  (set dsum decimal (add da db))
  (println (fmt "decimal: 0.1 + 0.2 = {}" (str dsum)))

  (println "--- chained: int -> float -> decimal -> int ---")
  (set origin 255)
  (set s1 (cast_int_float origin))
  (set s2 decimal (cast_float_decimal s1))
  (set s3 (cast_decimal_int s2))
  (println (fmt "int:{} -> float:{} -> decimal:{} -> int:{}" (str origin) (str s1) (str s2) (str s3)))

  (println "--- arithmetic requires explicit cast ---")
  (set base 7)
  (set divisor 2.0)
  (set result (div (cast_int_float base) divisor))
  (println (fmt "7 / 2.0 = {}" (str result)))

  (ret 0)
)

)

Count occurrences of a character (arg2) in a string (arg1).

(module char_counter

(fn count_char s string ch string -> int
  (set count 0)
  (set n (len s))
  (set ch_code (string_get ch 0))
  (for i 0 n
    (if (eq (string_get s i) ch_code)
      (set count (add count 1))))
  (ret count))

    (println "Usage: program <string> <character>")
    (ret 1))
  (set s $0)
  (set ch $1)
  (set result (count_char s ch))
  (println (fmt "Occurrences of '{}' in \"{}\": {}" ch s result))
  (ret 0))

)

(fn count_types s string -> array

    (cond

Write a Sigil program that implements edit distance.

(module edit_distance

(fn levenshtein s1 string s2 string -> int
  (set m (len s1))
  (set n (len s2))
  (set cols (add n 1))
  (set size (mul (add m 1) cols))
  (set dp [])
  (set k 0)
  (while (lt k size)
    (set dp (push dp 0))
    (set k (add k 1)))
  (for j 0 (add n 1)
    (set dp (array_set dp j j)))
  (for i 0 (add m 1)
    (set dp (array_set dp (mul i cols) i)))
  (for i 1 (add m 1)
    (for j 1 (add n 1)
      (set c1 (string_get s1 (sub i 1)))
      (set c2 (string_get s2 (sub j 1)))
      (set idx (add (mul i cols) j))
      (if (eq c1 c2)
        (set dp (array_set dp idx (array_get dp (add (mul (sub i 1) cols) (sub j 1)))))
        (else
          (set cost_del (array_get dp (add (mul (sub i 1) cols) j)))
          (set cost_ins (array_get dp (add (mul i cols) (sub j 1))))
          (set cost_rep (array_get dp (add (mul (sub i 1) cols) (sub j 1))))
          (set best (add 1 (min cost_del (min cost_ins cost_rep))))
          (set dp (array_set dp idx best))))))
  (ret (array_get dp (add (mul m cols) n))))

(fn show_pair s1 string s2 string -> unit
  (set d (levenshtein s1 s2))
  (println (fmt "levenshtein(\"{}\", \"{}\") = {}" s1 s2 d)))

(fn main -> int
  (show_pair "kitten" "sitting")
  (show_pair "saturday" "sunday")
  (show_pair "intention" "execution")
  (show_pair "" "abc")
  (show_pair "abc" "abc")
  (show_pair "abc" "")
  (show_pair "a" "b")))

  (ret result))

Group words by their first letter.

(module group_by_first_letter

(fn get_value pair string -> string
  (set pos (string_find pair "="))
  (if (lt pos 0)
    (ret ""))
  (set start (add pos 1))
  (set n (sub (len pair) start))
  (ret (string_slice pair start n)))

(fn main -> int
  (set args (argv))
  (set count (len args))
  (set groups {})
  (set pair "")
  (set val "")
  (set first "")
  (set existing [])
  (set grp [])
  (for i 0 count
    (set pair (array_get args i))
    (set val (get_value pair))
    (if (gt (len val) 0)
      (set first (string_slice val 0 1))
      (if (in first groups)
        (set existing (map_get groups first))
        (set existing (push existing val))
        (set groups (map_set groups first existing))
        (else
          (set grp [])
          (set grp (push grp val))
          (set groups (map_set groups first grp))))))
  (set letters (map_keys groups))
  (set letters (sort letters))
  (set num (len letters))
  (set letter "")
  (set vals [])
  (set joined "")
  (for j 0 num
    (set letter (array_get letters j))
    (set vals (map_get groups letter))
    (set joined (string_join vals ", "))
    (println (fmt "{}: {}" letter joined)))
  (ret 0))
)

  (set max_val 0)

Write a Sigil program that implements merge intervals.

(module merge_intervals

(fn sort_intervals intervals array -> array
  (set n (len intervals))
  (set i 0)
  (while (lt i (sub n 1))
    (set j 0)
    (while (lt j (sub (sub n 1) i))
      (set a (array_get intervals j))
      (set b (array_get intervals (add j 1)))
      (set a_start (array_get a 0))
      (set b_start (array_get b 0))
      (if (gt a_start b_start) (set intervals (array_set intervals j b)) (set intervals (array_set intervals (add j 1) a)) (else))
      (set j (add j 1)))
    (set i (add i 1)))
  (ret intervals))

(fn merge_sorted intervals array -> array
  (set result [])
  (set n (len intervals))
  (if (eq n 0) (ret result))
  (set first (array_get intervals 0))
  (set cur_start (array_get first 0))
  (set cur_end (array_get first 1))
  (set i 1)
  (while (lt i n)
    (set interval (array_get intervals i))
    (set start (array_get interval 0))
    (set end (array_get interval 1))
    (if (le start cur_end)
      (set cur_end (max cur_end end))
      (else
        (set merged [cur_start cur_end])
        (set result (push result merged))
        (set cur_start start)
        (set cur_end end)))
    (set i (add i 1)))
  (set last [cur_start cur_end])
  (set result (push result last))
  (ret result))

(fn print_intervals intervals array -> unit
  (set n (len intervals))
  (set i 0)
  (while (lt i n)
    (set interval (array_get intervals i))
    (set s (array_get interval 0))
    (set e (array_get interval 1))
    (println (fmt "[{}, {}]" s e))
    (set i (add i 1))))

(fn main -> int
  (set raw (stdin_read_all))
  (set lines (split raw "\n"))
  (set intervals [])
  (set i 0)
  (set n (len lines))
  (while (lt i n)
    (set line (trim (array_get lines i)))
    (if (gt (len line) 0)
      (set parts (split line " "))
      (set lo (int (array_get parts 0)))
      (set hi (int (array_get parts 1)))
      (set interval [lo hi])
      (set intervals (push intervals interval)))
    (set i (add i 1)))
  (set sorted (sort_intervals intervals))
  (set result (merge_sorted sorted))
  (println "Merged intervals:")
  (print_intervals result)
  (ret 0))

)

Take two SORTED ascending space-separated lists of integers. Print them merged into one sorted list, space-separated.

(module merge_sorted_lists

  (fn parse_list line string -> array
    (set parts (split line " "))
    (set result [])
    (set n (len parts))
    (for i 0 n
      (set part (array_get parts i))
      (set trimmed (trim part))
      (if (gt (len trimmed) 0)
        (set result (push result (int trimmed)))))
    (ret result))

  (fn merge arr1 array arr2 array -> array
    (set result [])
    (set i 0)
    (set j 0)
    (set len1 (len arr1))
    (set len2 (len arr2))
    (while (and (lt i len1) (lt j len2))
      (set a (array_get arr1 i))
      (set b (array_get arr2 j))
      (if (le a b)
        (set result (push result a))
        (set i (add i 1))
        (else
          (set result (push result b))
          (set j (add j 1)))))
    (while (lt i len1)
      (set result (push result (array_get arr1 i)))
      (set i (add i 1)))
    (while (lt j len2)
      (set result (push result (array_get arr2 j)))
      (set j (add j 1)))
    (ret result))

  (fn main -> int
    (set line1 (read_line))
    (set line2 (read_line))
    (set arr1 (parse_list line1))
    (set arr2 (parse_list line2))
    (set merged (merge arr1 arr2))
    (set n (len merged))
    (set out [])
    (for i 0 n
      (set out (push out (str (array_get merged i)))))
    (println (string_join out " "))
    (ret 0))
)

Write a Sigil program that implements mixed arithmetic.

(module mixed_arithmetic

(fn is_float_str s string -> bool
  (ret (string_contains s ".")))

    (ret (float s))
    (else
      (ret (cast_int_float (int s))))))

  (set n (argv_count))
  (if (lt n 2)
    (println "Usage: program <num1> <num2> [num3...]")
    (ret 1))

    (push vals (to_float (array_get (argv) i))))

  (set product 1.0)
  (set mn (array_get vals 0))
  (set mx (array_get vals 0))
  (for-each v float vals
    (set total (add total v))
    (set product (mul product v))
    (set mn (min mn v))
    (set mx (max mx v)))
  (set avg (div total (cast_int_float n)))
  (println (fmt "Count:   {}" n))
  (println (fmt "Sum:     {}" total))
  (println (fmt "Product: {}" product))
  (println (fmt "Min:     {}" mn))
  (println (fmt "Max:     {}" mx))
  (println (fmt "Average: {}" avg))
  (ret 0))
)

  (ret 0))
)

Write a Sigil program that implements rle.

(module rle

(fn encode s string -> string
  (set chars (string_chars s))
  (set result "")
  (set n (len chars))
  (if (eq n 0) (ret result))
  (set current (array_get chars 0))
  (set count 1)
  (for i 1 n
    (set ch (array_get chars i))
    (if (string_equals ch current)
      (set count (add count 1))
      (else
        (set result (add result (add (str count) current)))
        (set current ch)
        (set count 1))))
  (set result (add result (add (str count) current)))
  (ret result))

(fn decode s string -> string
  (set chars (string_chars s))
  (set result "")
  (set n (len chars))
  (set i 0)
  (while (lt i n)
    (set ch (array_get chars i))
    (cond
      ((is_digit ch)
        (set num_str "")
        (while (and (lt i n) (is_digit (array_get chars i)))
          (set num_str (add num_str (array_get chars i)))
          (set i (add i 1)))
        (if (lt i n)
          (set rep (array_get chars i))
          (set i (add i 1))
          (set count (int num_str))
          (for j 0 count
            (set result (add result rep)))))
      (true
        (set result (add result ch))
        (set i (add i 1)))))
  (ret result))

  (set e1 (encode s1))
  (println (fmt "Original: {}" s1))
  (println (fmt "Encoded:  {}" e1))
  (println (fmt "Decoded:  {}" (decode e1)))
  (println "")
  (set s2 "ABCDE")

  (println (fmt "Original: {}" s2))

  (println (fmt "Original: {}" s3))
  (println (fmt "Encoded:  {}" e3))
  (println (fmt "Decoded:  {}" (decode e3)))
  (ret 0))
)

(module rle_encoding

(fn encode s string -> string
  (set result "")
  (set n (len s))
  (if (eq n 0) (ret ""))
  (set current_char (string_get s 0))
  (set count 1)
  (for i 1 n
    (set c (string_get s i))
    (if (eq c current_char)
      (set count (add count 1))
      (else
        (set result (add result (str count)))
        (set result (add result (char_from_code current_char)))
        (set current_char c)

  (set result (add result (char_from_code current_char)))
  (ret result))

  (set i 0)
  (while (lt i n)

    (while (and (lt i n) (and (ge (string_get s i) 48) (le (string_get s i) 57)))
      (set count_str (add count_str (char_from_code (string_get s i))))
      (set i (add i 1)))
    (if (lt i n)
      (set count (int count_str))
      (else (ret result)))
    (set ch (char_from_code (string_get s i)))
    (set i (add i 1))
    (for j 0 count
      (set result (add result ch))))
  (ret result))

        (else (set result (add result curr))))
      (else (set result (add result curr)))))
  (ret result))

Evaluate a Reverse Polish Notation expression given as individual CLI argument tokens (integers and operators +, -, *, /, where / is integer floor division), then print the final top-of-stack integer result.

(module rpn_calculator

(fn evaluate expr string -> int
  (set stack [])
  (set tokens (split expr " "))
  (set n (len tokens))
  (for i 0 n
    (set token (array_get tokens i))
    (cond
      ((string_equals token "+")
        (set b (array_pop stack))
        (set a (array_pop stack))
        (push stack (add a b)))
      ((string_equals token "-")
        (set b (array_pop stack))
        (set a (array_pop stack))
        (push stack (sub a b)))
      ((string_equals token "*")
        (set b (array_pop stack))
        (set a (array_pop stack))
        (push stack (mul a b)))
      ((string_equals token "/")
        (set b (array_pop stack))
        (set a (array_pop stack))
        (push stack (div a b)))
      (true
        (push stack (int token)))))
  (ret (array_pop stack)))

(fn main -> int
  (set examples [])
  (push examples "3 4 +")
  (push examples "10 2 /")
  (push examples "3 4 + 2 *")
  (push examples "3 4 + 2 * 7 /")
  (push examples "5 1 2 + 4 * + 3 -")
  (push examples "15 7 1 1 + - / 3 * 2 1 1 + + -")
  (set n (len examples))
  (for i 0 n
    (set expr (array_get examples i))
    (set result (evaluate expr))
    (println (fmt "{} = {}" expr result)))
  (ret 0))

)

(module run_length_decode

(fn decode encoded string -> string
  (set result "")
  (set count_str "")
  (set n (len encoded))
  (set i 0)
  (while (lt i n)
    (set ch (string_get encoded i))
    (if (and (ge ch 48) (le ch 57))
      (set count_str (add count_str (char_from_code ch)))
      (else
        (set repeat_count 1)
        (if (gt (len count_str) 0)
          (set repeat_count (int count_str)))
        (set count_str "")
        (set ch_str (char_from_code ch))
        (set j 0)
        (while (lt j repeat_count)
          (set result (add result ch_str))
          (set j (add j 1)))))
    (set i (add i 1)))
  (ret result))

(fn main -> int

  (println (decode "1H1e2l1o"))
  (println (decode "10X"))
  (println (decode "2R3G4B"))
  (ret 0))
)

  (ret arr))

Rotate a string by N positions.

(module string_rotate

(fn rotate s string n int -> string
  (set n (len s))
  (if (eq n 0) (ret s))
  (set n (mod n n))
  (if (eq n 0) (ret s))
  (set part1 (string_slice s n (sub len n)))
  (set part2 (string_slice s 0 n))
  (ret (add part1 part2)))

(fn main -> int

  (println (rotate "abcdefg" 3))

  (println (rotate "hello" 7))
  (ret 0))
)

Write a Sigil program that implements substitution cipher.

(module substitution_cipher

  (fn encode text string -> string
    (set plain "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ")
    (set cipher "QWERTYUIOPASDFGHJKLZXCVBNM")
    (set result "")
    (set n (len text))
    (for i 0 n
      (set ch (string_slice text i 1))
      (set upper_ch (upper ch))
      (set idx (string_find plain upper_ch))
      (if (ge idx 0)
        (set mapped (string_slice cipher idx 1))
        (if (is_lower ch)
          (set mapped (lower mapped)))
        (set result (add result mapped))
        (else
          (set result (add result ch)))))
    (ret result))

  (fn decode text string -> string
    (set plain "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ")
    (set cipher "QWERTYUIOPASDFGHJKLZXCVBNM")
    (set result "")
    (set n (len text))
    (for i 0 n
      (set ch (string_slice text i 1))
      (set upper_ch (upper ch))
      (set idx (string_find cipher upper_ch))
      (if (ge idx 0)
        (set mapped (string_slice plain idx 1))
        (if (is_lower ch)
          (set mapped (lower mapped)))
        (set result (add result mapped))
        (else
          (set result (add result ch)))))
    (ret result))

  (fn main -> int
    (set original "Hello, World! The Quick Brown Fox.")
    (set encoded (encode original))
    (set decoded (decode encoded))
    (println (fmt "Original: {}" original))
    (println (fmt "Encoded:  {}" encoded))
    (println (fmt "Decoded:  {}" decoded))
    (if (string_equals original decoded)
      (println "Round-trip OK")
      (else
        (println "Round-trip FAILED")))
    (ret 0))
)

(module sum_of_digits
  (fn sum_digits n int -> int
    (set s (str (abs n)))
    (set n (len s))
    (set total 0)
    (set zero_code (string_get "0" 0))
    (for i 0 n
      (set digit (sub (string_get s i) zero_code))
      (set total (add total digit)))
    (ret total))

    (println result)
    (ret 0)))

Write a Sigil program that implements syllable counter.

(module syllable_counter

(fn is_vowel c int -> bool
  (ret (or (eq c 97) (or (eq c 101) (or (eq c 105) (or (eq c 111) (eq c 117)))))))

(fn count_syllables word string -> int
  (set lower (lower word))
  (set n (len lower))
  (set count 0)
  (set in_vowel false)
  (for i 0 n
    (set c (string_get lower i))
    (if (is_vowel c)
      (if (not in_vowel) (set count (add count 1)) (set in_vowel true) (else))
      (else
        (set in_vowel false))))
  (if (eq count 0)
    (set count 1))
  (ret count))

(fn main -> int
  (println (fmt "hello -> {}" (count_syllables "hello")))
  (println (fmt "beautiful -> {}" (count_syllables "beautiful")))
  (println (fmt "rhythm -> {}" (count_syllables "rhythm")))
  (println (fmt "a -> {}" (count_syllables "a")))
  (println (fmt "strength -> {}" (count_syllables "strength")))
  (println (fmt "education -> {}" (count_syllables "education")))
  (println (fmt "programming -> {}" (count_syllables "programming")))
  (ret 0))
)

Write a Sigil program that implements t3 stack calc.

(set toks (split $0 " "))
(set stack [])
(for-each t toks
  (cond
    ((eq t "+") (set b (array_pop stack)) (set a (array_pop stack)) (push stack (add a b)))
    ((eq t "-") (set b (array_pop stack)) (set a (array_pop stack)) (push stack (sub a b)))
    ((eq t "*") (set b (array_pop stack)) (set a (array_pop stack)) (push stack (mul a b)))
    ((eq t "/") (set b (array_pop stack)) (set a (array_pop stack)) (push stack (div a b)))
    (true (push stack (int t)))))
(println (array_get stack -1))

Write a Sigil program that implements timestamp gap.

(module timestamp_gap
  (fn parse_time s string -> int
    (set parts (split s ":"))
    (if (lt (len parts) 2) (ret 0))
    (set hours (int (array_get parts 0)))
    (set minutes (int (array_get parts 1)))
    (ret (add (mul hours 60) minutes)))

  (fn main -> int
    (set n (argv_count))
    (if (lt n 2)
      (println "Usage: timestamp_gap HH:MM HH:MM ...")
      (ret 1))
    (set times [])
    (for i 0 n
      (push times (parse_time (array_get (argv) i))))
    (sort times)
    (set min_gap 1440)
    (for i 0 (sub (len times) 1)
      (set gap (sub (array_get times (add i 1)) (array_get times i)))
      (if (lt gap min_gap)
        (set min_gap gap)))
    (set wrap (add (sub 1440 (last times)) (array_get times 0)))
    (if (lt wrap min_gap)
      (set min_gap wrap))
    (println min_gap)
    (ret 0)))

Write a Sigil program that implements timestamps min gap.

(module timestamps_min_gap

(fn parse_time s string -> int
  (set parts (split s ":"))
  (set h (int (array_get parts 0)))
  (set m (int (array_get parts 1)))
  (ret (add (mul h 60) m)))

(fn find_min_gap minutes array -> int
  (set sorted (sort minutes))
  (set n (len sorted))
  (set min_gap 1440)
  (for i 1 n
    (set a (array_get sorted (sub i 1)))
    (set b (array_get sorted i))
    (set gap (sub b a))
    (if (lt gap min_gap)
      (set min_gap gap)))
  (set first (array_get sorted 0))
  (set last (array_get sorted (sub n 1)))
  (set wrap_gap (add (sub 1440 last) first))
  (if (lt wrap_gap min_gap)
    (set min_gap wrap_gap))
  (ret min_gap))

(fn format_gap minutes int -> string
  (set h (div minutes 60))
  (set m (mod minutes 60))
  (ret (fmt "{}h {}m ({} min)" h m minutes)))

(fn main -> int
  (set count (argv_count))
  (if (lt count 2)
    (println "Usage: program HH:MM HH:MM ... (at least 2 timestamps required)")
    (ret 1))
  (set minutes [])  (for-each ts string args
    (set minutes (push minutes (parse_time ts))))
  (set min_gap (find_min_gap minutes))
  (println (fmt "Smallest gap: {}" (format_gap min_gap)))
  (ret 0))
)

Write a Sigil program that implements unique values.

(module unique_values

(fn get_unique parts array -> array
  (set seen {})
  (set result [])
  (set n (len parts))
  (for i 0 n
    (set part (array_get parts i))
    (set trimmed (trim part))
    (if (and (gt (len trimmed) 0) (not (in trimmed seen)))
      (set seen (map_set seen trimmed true))
      (set result (push result trimmed))))
  (ret result))

(fn main -> int  (set all_input (string_join args " "))
  (set parts (split all_input " "))
  (set unique (get_unique parts))
  (set ulen (len unique))
  (for i 0 ulen
    (println (array_get unique i)))
  (ret 0))
)

Parse a full URL with userinfo, port, path, query, and fragment and print each component on its own labeled line in the fixed order: scheme, user, pass, host, port, path, query, fragment.

(module url_parser

(fn parse_url url string -> unit
  (set proto_sep (string_find url "://"))
  (if (eq proto_sep -1)
    (println "Error: no protocol separator found")
    (else
      (set protocol (string_slice url 0 proto_sep))
      (set rest_start (add proto_sep 3))
      (set rest_len (sub (len url) rest_start))
      (set after_proto (string_slice url rest_start rest_len))
      (set slash_pos (string_find after_proto "/"))
      (set host "")
      (set path "/")
      (if (eq slash_pos -1)
        (set host after_proto)
        (else
          (set host (string_slice after_proto 0 slash_pos))
          (set path (string_slice after_proto slash_pos (sub (len after_proto) slash_pos)))
        )
      )
      (println (fmt "Protocol: {}" protocol))
      (println (fmt "Host:     {}" host))
      (println (fmt "Path:     {}" path))
    )
  )
)

(fn main -> int  (if (lt (len args) 1)
    (println "Usage: url_parser <url>")
    (else
      (set url $0)
      (parse_url url)
    )
  )
  (ret 0)
)

)

Write a Sigil program that implements validate positive int.

(module validate_positive_int

(fn is_valid_positive_int s string -> bool
  (set trimmed (trim s))
  (if (eq (len trimmed) 0)
    (ret false))
  (set n (len trimmed))
  (set i 0)
  (while (lt i n)
    (set ch (string_slice trimmed i 1))
    (if (not (is_digit ch))
      (ret false))
    (set i (add i 1)))
  (set val (int trimmed))
  (ret (gt val 0)))

(fn main -> int
  (print "Enter a value: ")
  (set line (read_line))
  (if (is_valid_positive_int line)
    (println (fmt "Valid positive integer: {}" (trim line)))
    (else
      (println "Error: input must be a positive integer")))
  (ret 0))
)

Write a Sigil program that implements version compare.

(module version_compare

(fn parse_version v string -> array
  (ret (split v ".")))

(fn compare_versions a string b string -> int
  (set parts_a (parse_version a))
  (set parts_b (parse_version b))
  (set len_a (len parts_a))
  (set len_b (len parts_b))
  (set max_len (max len_a len_b))
  (set i 0)
  (set result 0)
  (set num_a 0)
  (set num_b 0)
  (while (and (lt i max_len) (eq result 0))
    (set num_a 0)
    (set num_b 0)
    (if (lt i len_a)
      (set num_a (int (array_get parts_a i))))
    (if (lt i len_b)
      (set num_b (int (array_get parts_b i))))
    (cond
      ((lt num_a num_b) (set result -1))
      ((gt num_a num_b) (set result 1))
      (true (set result 0)))
    (set i (add i 1)))
  (ret result))

(fn main -> int
  (set v1 $0)
  (set v2 $1)
  (set cmp (compare_versions v1 v2))
  (cond
    ((lt cmp 0) (println (fmt "{} is newer" v2)))
    ((gt cmp 0) (println (fmt "{} is newer" v1)))
    (true (println (fmt "{} and {} are equal" v1 v2))))
  (ret 0))

)

For each space-separated word in arg1 (in order), print the word left-padded to the longest word's length, then ' | ', then '#' repeated for the word's length.

(module word_length_histogram

(fn build_bar count int -> string
  (set bar "")
  (for i 0 count
    (set bar (add bar "#")))
  (ret bar))

(fn main -> int
  (println "Enter a sentence:")
  (set sentence (read_line))
  (set words (split sentence " "))
  (set freq {})
  (set num_words (len words))
  (set max_len 0)
  (set wlen 0)
  (set key "")
  (set word "")
  (for i 0 num_words
    (set word (array_get words i))
    (set wlen (len word))
    (if (gt wlen 0)
      (set key (str wlen))
      (if (in key freq)
        (set freq (map_set freq key (add (map_get freq key) 1)))
        (else (set freq (map_set freq key 1))))
      (if (gt wlen max_len)
        (set max_len wlen))))
  (println "Word Length Histogram:")
  (set count 0)
  (set bar "")
  (for i 1 (add max_len 1)
    (set key (str i))
    (if (in key freq)
      (set count (map_get freq key))
      (set bar (build_bar count))
      (println (fmt "{}: {} ({})" key bar count))))
  (ret 0))
)

Wrap a sentence at width N (arg2), preserving word boundaries.

(module word_wrap

(fn wrap words array width int -> string
  (set result "")
  (set line "")
  (set count (len words))
  (for i 0 count
    (set word (array_get words i))
    (if (eq (len word) 0)
      (continue))
    (cond
      ((eq (len line) 0)
       (set line word))
      ((le (add (add (len line) 1) (len word)) width)
       (set line (fmt "{} {}" line word)))
      (true
       (set result (fmt "{}{}\n" result line))
       (set line word))))
  (if (gt (len line) 0)
    (set result (add result line)))
  (ret result))

(fn main -> int
  (if (lt (argv_count) 1)
    (println "Usage: word_wrap <width>")
    (ret 1))
  (set width #0)
  (set text (stdin_read_all))
  (set normalized (string_replace text "\n" " "))
  (set words (split normalized " "))
  (set output (wrap words width))
  (println output)
  (ret 0))
)

Write a Sigil program that implements xor encode.

(module xor_encode

(fn xor_encode_string s string key_char string -> string
  (set result "")
  (set key_code (string_get key_char 0))
  (set chars (string_chars s))
  (for-each ch string chars
    (set code (string_get ch 0))
    (set xored (bit_xor code key_code))
    (set result (add result (char_from_code xored))))
  (ret result))

  (println "Input:")

  (set encoded (xor_encode_string input key))
  (println "Encoded:")
  (println encoded)
  (set decoded (xor_encode_string encoded key))
  (println "Decoded:")
  (println decoded)
  (ret 0))

(module test_base64
  (import base64)

  (fn test_encode data string -> string
    (ret (base64_encode data)))

  (fn test_decode encoded string -> string
    (ret (base64_decode encoded)))

  (fn test_roundtrip data string -> string
    (set encoded (base64_encode data))
    (set decoded (base64_decode encoded))
    (ret decoded))

  (test-spec test_encode
    (case "encode empty string"
      (input "")
      (expect ""))
    (case "encode single char"
      (input "A")
      (expect "QQ=="))
    (case "encode two chars"
      (input "AB")
      (expect "QUI="))
    (case "encode three chars"
      (input "ABC")
      (expect "QUJD"))
    (case "encode Hello"
      (input "Hello")
      (expect "SGVsbG8="))
    (case "encode Hello, World!"
      (input "Hello, World!")
      (expect "SGVsbG8sIFdvcmxkIQ==")))

  (test-spec test_decode
    (case "decode empty string"
      (input "")
      (expect ""))
    (case "decode single char with padding"
      (input "QQ==")
      (expect "A"))
    (case "decode two chars with padding"
      (input "QUI=")
      (expect "AB"))
    (case "decode three chars no padding"
      (input "QUJD")
      (expect "ABC"))
    (case "decode Hello"
      (input "SGVsbG8=")
      (expect "Hello"))
    (case "decode Hello, World!"
      (input "SGVsbG8sIFdvcmxkIQ==")
      (expect "Hello, World!")))

  (test-spec test_roundtrip
    (case "roundtrip simple text"
      (input "The quick brown fox")
      (expect "The quick brown fox"))
    (case "roundtrip with special chars"
      (input "key=value&foo=bar")
      (expect "key=value&foo=bar")))

  (meta-note "Tests base64 encoding and decoding with padding variants and roundtrip"))

        (set result "C"))

    (cond

Write a Sigil test that verifies: empty table returns zero rows.

(module test_sqlite_empty
  (import sqlite)
  
  (fn query_empty_table -> int
    (set db process (open ":memory:"))
    (exec db "CREATE TABLE test (id INTEGER, val TEXT);")
    (set rows (query db "SELECT * FROM test;"))
    (set count (len rows))
    (close db)
    (ret count))
  
  (fn query_with_rows -> int
    (set db process (open ":memory:"))
    (exec db "CREATE TABLE test (id INTEGER, val TEXT);")
    (exec db "INSERT INTO test VALUES (1, 'Alice');")
    (exec db "INSERT INTO test VALUES (2, 'Bob');")
    (set rows (query db "SELECT * FROM test;"))
    (set count (len rows))
    (close db)
    (ret count))
  
  (fn query_first_row -> string
    (set db process (open ":memory:"))
    (exec db "CREATE TABLE test (id INTEGER, val TEXT);")
    (exec db "INSERT INTO test VALUES (1, 'Alice');")
    (set rows (query db "SELECT * FROM test;"))
    (set first_row (array_get rows 0))
    (set val (array_get first_row 1))
    (close db)
    (ret val))
  
  (test-spec query_empty_table
    (case "empty table returns zero rows"
      (input)
      (expect 0)))
  
  (test-spec query_with_rows
    (case "table with 2 rows returns count 2"
      (input)
      (expect 2)))
  
  (test-spec query_first_row
    (case "retrieves first row value correctly"
      (input)
      (expect "Alice")))
  
  (meta-note "Tests SQLite empty query result handling.
  
  This validates the fix for the bug where empty SQLite query results returned
  an array of length 1 containing garbage data (typically 'j' character from
  uninitialized memory) instead of an empty array.
  
  The issue was that split on an empty string returns an array with one
  empty string element. The fix checks if the output string is empty before
  splitting and immediately returns an empty array.
  
  Critical test case is query_empty_table which ensures empty queries return
  array length 0 (not 1 with garbage data)."))

Write a Sigil test that verifies: passes empty string.

(module test_string_params
  (fn identity text string -> string
    (ret text))
  
  (fn concat_twice a string b string -> string
    (set result (add a b))
    (ret result))
  
  (fn chain text string -> string
    (set temp (identity text))
    (set result (identity temp))
    (ret result))
  
  (test-spec identity
    (case "passes empty string"
      (input "")
      (expect ""))
    (case "passes simple string"
      (input "hello")
      (expect "hello"))
    (case "passes string with spaces"
      (input "hello world")
      (expect "hello world")))
  
  (test-spec concat_twice
    (case "concatenates two strings"
      (input "hello" " world")
      (expect "hello world"))
    (case "concatenates with empty"
      (input "test" "")
      (expect "test")))
  
  (test-spec chain
    (case "passes string through multiple function calls"
      (input "test")
      (expect "test"))
    (case "passes empty string through chain"
      (input "")
      (expect "")))
  
  (meta-note "Tests string parameter passing through multiple function calls.
  
  This validates the fix for the double-free crash that occurred when strings
  were passed as parameters through multiple function calls. The issue was that
  OP_RETURN freed ALL locals including parameters, causing a double-free when
  the caller returned and tried to free the same memory.
  
  The fix tracks param_count in Function metadata and CallFrame, ensuring only
  non-parameter locals are freed on function return."))

(module test_try_catch
  (fn catch_div_zero -> string
    (set result "no error")
    (try
      (set x (div 10 0))
      (set result (str x))
      (catch err string
        (set result "caught")))
    (ret result))

  (fn try_no_error -> int
    (set result 0)
    (try
      (set result (add 10 20))
      (catch err string
        (set result -1)))
    (ret result))

  (fn catch_error_message -> string
    (set msg "none")
    (try
      (set x (div 5 0))
      (set msg (str x))
      (catch err string
        (set msg err)))
    (ret msg))

  (fn nested_try_catch -> string
    (set result "none")
    (try
      (try
        (set x (div 1 0))
        (set result (str x))
        (catch inner_err string
          (set result "inner")))
      (catch outer_err string
        (set result "outer")))
    (ret result))

  (fn try_catch_array_bounds -> string
    (set result "no error")
    (set arr [])
    (push arr 10)
    (try
      (set val (array_get arr 5))
      (set result (str val))
      (catch err string
        (set result "caught bounds")))
    (ret result))

  (test-spec catch_div_zero
    (case "catches division by zero"
      (input)
      (expect "caught")))

  (test-spec try_no_error
    (case "try body succeeds without error"
      (input)
      (expect 30)))

  (test-spec catch_error_message
    (case "error message is accessible in catch"
      (input)
      (expect "Division by zero")))

  (test-spec nested_try_catch
    (case "inner try catches error first"
      (input)
      (expect "inner")))

  (test-spec try_catch_array_bounds
    (case "catches array index out of bounds"
      (input)
      (expect "caught bounds")))

  (meta-note "Tests try/catch error handling: catching runtime errors, no-error path, error messages, nesting, and array bounds"))

Print the most frequent word in arg1; ties broken by first occurrence.

(set counts {})
(for-each w words (map_inc counts w))

Write a Sigil program that implements algo to hex.

(fn to_hex n int -> string
  (if (eq n 0) (ret "0"))
  (set digits "0123456789abcdef")
  (set result "")
  (while (gt n 0)
    (set d (mod n 16))
    (set result (string_concat (string_slice digits d 1) result))
    (set n (div n 16)))
  (ret result))

(println (to_hex #0))

Group the space-separated words into anagram groups; print each group on one line as comma-separated words sorted alphabetically, with groups ordered by size descending and ties broken by the first original word.

(set input $0)
(set words (split input " "))
(set groups {})
(set first_idx {})
(set i 0)
(for-each w words
  (set key (join (sort (chars w)) ""))
  (if (in key groups)
    (set existing (map_get groups key))
    (else
      (set existing [])
      (set first_idx (map_set first_idx key i))
    )
  )
  (set existing (push existing w))
  (set groups (map_set groups key existing))
  (set i (add i 1))
)
(set rows [])
(for-each k (map_keys groups)
  (set ws (sort (map_get groups k)))
  (set fi (map_get first_idx k))
  (set sz (len ws))
  (set rows (push rows [(add (neg (mul 1000000 sz)) fi) ws]))
)
(set rows (sort_by rows (\r (array_get r 0))))
(for-each r rows
  (println (join (array_get r 1) ","))
)

For each argument (an arithmetic expression of digits, +-*/ and parens), produce a token list where digits become 'NUM:value', operators become 'OP:c', '(' becomes 'LP', ')' becomes 'RP'; print 'INPUT -> [t1, t2, ...]'.

(fn tokenize s string -> array
  (set tokens [])
  (set num "")
  (set cs (chars s))
  (set n (len cs))
  (for i 0 n
    (set c (array_get cs i))
    (if (is_digit c)
      (set num (string_concat num c))
      (else
        (if (gt (len num) 0)
          (push tokens (string_concat "NUM:" num)))
        (set num "")
        (cond
          ((eq c "(") (push tokens "LP"))
          ((eq c ")") (push tokens "RP"))
          (true (push tokens (string_concat "OP:" c)))))))
  (if (gt (len num) 0)

For each CLI arg, print the string followed by 'OK' if parentheses, brackets, and braces are balanced and properly nested, otherwise 'BAD'.

(fn balanced s string -> bool
  (set stack [])
  (set pairs {")" "(" "]" "[" "}" "{"})
  (for-each c (chars s)
    (cond
      ((in c ["(" "[" "{"]) (push stack c))
      ((in c [")" "]" "}"])
        (if (eq (len stack) 0) (ret false))
        (if (ne (array_pop stack) (map_get pairs c)) (ret false)))))
  (ret (eq (len stack) 0)))

(for-each s (argv)
  (if (balanced s)
    (println (fmt "{} OK" s))
    (else (println (fmt "{} BAD" s)))))

Checks if the input string of brackets is balanced and outputs 'Balanced' or 'Not Balanced'.

(fn expected_close opener str -> str
  (cond
    ((eq opener "(") (ret ")"))
    ((eq opener "[") (ret "]"))
    ((eq opener "{") (ret "}"))
    (true (ret ""))))

(set stack [])
(set ok true)
(for-each c (chars $0)
  (if ok
    (cond
      ((in c ["(" "[" "{"]) (set stack (push stack c)))
      ((in c [")" "]" "}"])
        (cond
          ((eq (len stack) 0) (set ok false))
          ((eq (expected_close (last stack)) c) (set stack (slice stack 0 (sub (len stack) 1))))
          (true (set ok false)))))))
(cond
  ((and ok (eq (len stack) 0)) (println "Balanced"))
  (true (println "Not Balanced")))

Read a comma-separated list of integers, insert them in order into a BST, then print a preorder traversal one value per line.

(fn insert node map v int -> map
  (if (eq (len node) 0)
    (ret {"val" v "left" {} "right" {}}))
  (if (lt v (map_get node "val"))
    (ret (map_set node "left" (insert (map_get node "left") v)))
    (else
      (ret (map_set node "right" (insert (map_get node "right") v))))))

(fn preorder node map -> unit
  (if (gt (len node) 0)
    (println (map_get node "val"))
    (preorder (map_get node "left"))
    (preorder (map_get node "right"))))

  (set root (insert root (int p))))
(preorder root)

(fn lstrip s string -> string
  (cond
    ((eq (len s) 0) (ret s))
    ((or (eq (string_get s 0) 32) (or (eq (string_get s 0) 9) (or (eq (string_get s 0) 13) (eq (string_get s 0) 10))))

(set defs (len (filter stripped (\l (string_starts_with l "def ")))))
(set imports (len (filter stripped (\l (or (string_starts_with l "import ") (string_starts_with l "from "))))))

(println (fmt "total_lines: {}" total))

Take a string. After each character print the running count of occurrences of that character so far in the string, space-separated.

(set s string $0)
(set n int (len s))
(set out "")

  (set c string (string_slice s i 1))
  (set count int 0)
  (for j 0 (add i 1)
    (if (eq (string_slice s j 1) c)

    (set out (fmt "{} " out)))
  (set out (fmt "{}{}" out count)))

Print the absolute number of days between two ISO-format dates as '<n> days'.

(fn jdn s string -> int
  (set y (int (string_slice s 0 4)))
  (set m (int (string_slice s 5 2)))
  (set d (int (string_slice s 8 2)))
  (set a (div (sub 14 m) 12))
  (set yy (sub (add y 4800) a))
  (set mm (sub (add m (mul 12 a)) 3))
  (set t1 (div (add (mul 153 mm) 2) 5))
  (set t2 (mul 365 yy))
  (set t3 (div yy 4))
  (set t4 (div yy 100))
  (set t5 (div yy 400))
  (ret (sub (add (add (add (add d t1) t2) t3) (sub t5 t4)) 32045)))

(println (fmt "{} days" (abs (sub (jdn $1) (jdn $0)))))

  (set era (div y 400))
  (set yoe (sub y (mul era 400)))
  (set ma 0)
  (if (gt m 2) (set ma (sub m 3)) (else (set ma (add m 9))))
  (set doy (add (div (add (mul 153 ma) 2) 5) (sub d 1)))
  (set doe (add (sub (add (mul yoe 365) (div yoe 4)) (div yoe 100)) doy))
  (ret (add (mul era 146097) (sub doe 719468))))

(fn parse_date s string -> int
  (set p (split s "-"))
  (ret (days (int (array_get p 0)) (int (array_get p 1)) (int (array_get p 2)))))

(set s1 $0)

(println (fmt "Days between {s1} and {s2}: {diff}"))

(fn is_leap y int -> int
  (if (and (eq (mod y 4) 0) (or (ne (mod y 100) 0) (eq (mod y 400) 0)))
    (ret 1)
    (ret 0)))

(fn month_days m int leap int -> int
  (cond
    ((eq m 2) (ret (+ 28 leap)))
    ((or (eq m 4) (or (eq m 6) (or (eq m 9) (eq m 11)))) (ret 30))
    (true (ret 31))))

(fn date_days d str -> int
  (set parts (split d "-"))
  (set y (first (parse_ints (first parts))))
  (set m (first (parse_ints (first (rest parts)))))
  (set day (first (parse_ints (last parts))))
  (set leap (is_leap y))
  (set doy (+ (sum (map_arr (range 1 m) (\mo (month_days mo leap)))) day))
  (set total (- (+ (+ (+ (* 365 y) (/ y 4)) (/ y 400)) doy) (/ y 100)))
  (ret total))

Compare two newline-separated file blobs (argv[1] = original, argv[2] = modified), print lines removed from the original prefixed with '-' and lines added in the modified prefixed with '+' in diff order, then print '+N -M' with the total added and removed counts.

  (set s (str n))
  (set L (len s))
  (ret (reduce (enumerate (chars s)) (\(acc p) (cond ((and (gt (first p) 0) (eq (mod (sub L (first p)) 3) 0)) (join [acc "," (last p)] "")) (true (join [acc (last p)] "")))) "")))

(fn add_commas s string -> string
  (set n (len s))
  (set out "")
  (for-each i (range n)
    (set sep (cond
      ((and (gt i 0) (eq (mod (sub n i) 3) 0)) ",")
      (true "")))
    (set c (char_from_code (string_get s i)))
    (set out (join [out sep c] "")))
  (ret out))

Average (2 decimals) of values strictly greater than arg2 from arg1 (space-separated floats).

(set parts (split $0 " "))
(set threshold (float $1))
(set vals (map_arr parts (\x (float x))))
(set filtered (filter vals (\x (gt x threshold))))
(set total (sum filtered))
(set n (len filtered))
(set avg (div total (float n)))
(set scaled (round (mul avg 100.0)))
(set whole (div scaled 100))
(set frac (mod scaled 100))
(set frac_str (str frac))
(if (lt frac 10) (set frac_str (string_concat "0" frac_str)))
(println (fmt "{}.{}" whole frac_str))

Print average word length (2 decimals) of words in arg1.

(set words (split $0 " "))
(set total (sum (map_arr words (\w (len w)))))
(set count (len words))
(set avg (div (float total) (float count)))
(set scaled (round (mul avg 100.0)))
(set ip (div scaled 100))
(set fp (mod scaled 100))
(set fs (str fp))
(if (lt fp 10) (set fs (string_concat "0" fs)))
(println (fmt "{}.{}" ip fs))

Take a non-negative integer byte count in arg0. Print it as 'X.YZ B/KB/MB/GB/TB' with 2 decimal places, choosing the largest unit where the value is >= 1.

(set n int #0)
(if (le n 1024)
  (println (fmt "{} B" n))
  (else
    (set units array ["KB" "MB" "GB" "TB"])
    (set i int 0)
    (set val float (/ (cast_int_float n) 1024.0))
    (while (and (ge val 1024.0) (lt i 3))
      (set val (div val 1024.0))
      (set i (add i 1)))
    (println (fmt "{:.2f} {}" val (array_get units i)))))

Given an INI-style key=value blob (lines beginning with '#' are comments and are skipped) as argv[1] and a comma-separated list of key names as argv[2], print 'key=value' for each requested key in the order the keys were requested.

(set config map {})
(set keys array (split $1 ","))

(for-each line string (split $0 "\n")
  (if (and (not (in "#" (first line))) (> (len line) 0))
    (set parts array (split line "="))
    (set key string (array_get parts 0))
    (set value string (join (slice parts 1) "="))
    (set config (merge config {key value}))))

(for-each key string keys
  (if (in key config)
    (println (fmt "{}={}" key (get config key)))))

Parse a configuration string of key=value lines (skipping lines that are empty, start with '#', or start with '['), look up only the keys listed comma-separated in the second arg, and print each found pair as 'key=value' sorted alphabetically by key

(fn valid_line line string -> bool
  (cond
    ((eq (len line) 0) (ret false))
    ((string_starts_with line "#") (ret false))
    ((string_starts_with line "[") (ret false))
    (true (ret true))))
(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(set valid (filter lines valid_line))
(set wanted (split $1 ","))
(set found (filter valid (\line (in (first (split line "=")) wanted))))
(set sorted (sort_by found (\line (first (split line "=")))))
(for-each line sorted
  (println line))

Parse a key=value config string (argv[1]), ignoring lines starting with '#' and blank lines, splitting each entry on the first '='; then for each comma-separated key in argv[2] print 'key=value' if found or 'key=NOT_FOUND' if absent.

(set valid (filter (split $0 "\n") (\l (and (gt (len l) 0) (eq (string_starts_with l "#") false)))))
(for-each key (split $1 ",")
  (set matches (filter valid (\l (eq (first (split l "=")) key))))
  (cond
    ((gt (len matches) 0) (println (first matches)))
    (true (println (join [key "=NOT_FOUND"] "")))))

Parse a multi-section INI config string (argv[1]) and for each dot-separated key in a comma-separated lookup list (argv[2]) print 'section.key=value' in the same order as the lookup list.

(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(set config (json_parse "{}"))
(set current_section "")
(for-each line lines
  (if (string_starts_with line "[")
    (set current_section (first (split (last (split line "[")) "]"))))
  (if (string_contains line " = ")
    (set config (json_set config [(fmt "{}.{}" current_section (first (split line " = ")))] (last (split line " = "))))))
(for-each lookup (split $1 ",")
  (println (fmt "{}={}" lookup (json_get config [lookup]))))

Given an INI-format config blob as arg 0 with '[section]' headers and 'key=value' lines (blank lines and '#' comment lines allowed) and a section name as arg 1, print all key=value lines from that section in the order they appear

(set config string $0)
(set section string $1)
(set lines array (split config "\n"))
(set in_section bool false)

(for-each line string lines
  (if (and (not (eq (slice line 0 1) "#")) (not (eq line "")))
    (cond
      ((and (eq (slice line 0 1) "[") (eq (slice line (- (len line) 1) (len line)) "]"))
        (set section_name string (slice line 1 (- (len line) 1)))
        (set in_section bool (eq section_name section)))
      (in_section
        (println line)))))

Convert an integer (1..3999) to its Roman numeral representation using greedy subtraction with the standard symbol table.

(fn to_roman n int -> str
  (cond
    ((ge n 1000) (ret (join ["M" (to_roman (sub n 1000))] "")))
    ((ge n 900) (ret (join ["CM" (to_roman (sub n 900))] "")))
    ((ge n 500) (ret (join ["D" (to_roman (sub n 500))] "")))
    ((ge n 400) (ret (join ["CD" (to_roman (sub n 400))] "")))
    ((ge n 100) (ret (join ["C" (to_roman (sub n 100))] "")))
    ((ge n 90) (ret (join ["XC" (to_roman (sub n 90))] "")))
    ((ge n 50) (ret (join ["L" (to_roman (sub n 50))] "")))
    ((ge n 40) (ret (join ["XL" (to_roman (sub n 40))] "")))
    ((ge n 10) (ret (join ["X" (to_roman (sub n 10))] "")))
    ((ge n 9) (ret (join ["IX" (to_roman (sub n 9))] "")))
    ((ge n 5) (ret (join ["V" (to_roman (sub n 5))] "")))
    ((ge n 4) (ret (join ["IV" (to_roman (sub n 4))] "")))
    ((ge n 1) (ret (join ["I" (to_roman (sub n 1))] "")))
    (true (ret ""))))
(println (to_roman #0))

Given a grid (rows separated by \n) of '#' (land) and '.' (water), count 4-connected '#' islands using DFS and print 'islands: N'.

(fn dfs rows_arr array visited array r int c int rows int cols int -> int
  (if (lt r 0) (ret 0))
  (if (ge r rows) (ret 0))
  (if (lt c 0) (ret 0))
  (if (ge c cols) (ret 0))
  (set idx (add (mul r cols) c))
  (if (eq (array_get visited idx) 1) (ret 0))
  (set row (array_get rows_arr r))
  (if (ne (string_get row c) 35) (ret 0))
  (array_set visited idx 1)
  (dfs rows_arr visited (sub r 1) c rows cols)
  (dfs rows_arr visited (add r 1) c rows cols)
  (dfs rows_arr visited r (sub c 1) rows cols)
  (dfs rows_arr visited r (add c 1) rows cols)
  (ret 0))

(set raw $0)
(set rows_arr (split raw "\n"))
(set rows (len rows_arr))
(set cols (len (first rows_arr)))
(set visited [])
(set total (mul rows cols))
(for i 0 total (push visited 0))
(set count 0)
(for r 0 rows
  (for c 0 cols
    (set idx (add (mul r cols) c))
    (if (eq (array_get visited idx) 0)
      (if (eq (string_get (array_get rows_arr r) c) 35)
        (set count (add count 1))
        (dfs rows_arr visited r c rows cols)))))
(println (fmt "islands: {}" count))

  (set n (len s))

  (cond
    ((is_array j)
      (for-each elem j
        (walk elem path idx)))
    ((ge idx (len path))
      (print_leaf j))
    (true
      (set key (array_get path idx))
      (if (json_has j key)

Parse the JSON object and print each leaf path as 'dotted.key=value' lines, sorted alphabetically by key.

(fn build_prefix prefix string k string -> string
  (if (eq (len prefix) 0)
    (ret k))
  (ret (fmt "{}.{}" prefix k)))

(fn collect j json prefix string acc array -> array
  (cond
    ((is_object j)
      (for-each k (map_keys j)
        (set new_prefix (build_prefix prefix k))
        (set acc (collect (json_get j k) new_prefix acc)))
      (ret acc))
    (true
      (ret (push acc (fmt "{}={}" prefix (json_stringify j)))))))

Parse a JSON array of product objects with 'name', 'category', and 'price' fields from argv[1], keep items whose price strictly exceeds the float threshold in argv[2], sort by price descending, print each as 'name: $X.XX', then print '---' and a count per category in alphabetical order.

(fn fmt_price p float -> string
  (set cents int (round (mul p 100.0)))
  (set whole int (div cents 100))
  (set frac int (mod cents 100))
  (set frac_str string (string_from_int frac))
  (if (lt frac 10)
    (set frac_str (string_concat "0" frac_str)))
  (ret (string_format "{}.{}" whole frac_str)))

(set j json (json_parse $0))
(set threshold float (string_to_float $1))
(set items array [])
(set n int (json_length j))
(for i 0 n
  (set obj (json_get j i))
  (set price float (string_to_float (str (json_get obj "price"))))
  (set pname string (str (json_get obj "name")))
  (set pcat string (str (json_get obj "category")))
  (if (gt price threshold)
    (set entry array [price pname pcat])
    (set items (push items entry))))

(set m int (array_length items))
(for i 0 m
  (set bound int (sub (sub m i) 1))
  (for jj 0 bound
    (set a (array_get items jj))
    (set b (array_get items (add jj 1)))
    (if (lt (array_get a 0) (array_get b 0)) (set items (array_set items jj b)) (set items (array_set items (add jj 1) a)) (else))))

(set counts map {})
(for-each item items
  (set ecat string (array_get item 2))
  (set ename string (array_get item 1))
  (set eprice float (array_get item 0))
  (println (string_format "{}: ${}" ename (fmt_price eprice)))
  (if (map_has counts ecat)
    (set counts (map_set counts ecat (add (map_get counts ecat) 1)))
    (else (set counts (map_set counts ecat 1)))))

(println "---")

(set keys (map_keys counts))
(set keys (sort keys))
(for-each k string keys
  (println (string_format "{}: {}" k (map_get counts k))))

Given a JSON array of objects each with 'name' (string), 'score' (int), and 'dept' (string) fields (arg0), keep objects with score >= int(arg1), sort by score descending then name ascending, print each as 'N. name (dept): score' numbered from 1, then print '---' followed by per-department counts sorted alphabetically as 'dept: count'.

(set data (json_parse $0))
(set filtered (filter data (\item (ge (json_get item ["score"]) #1))))
(set sorted (sort_by filtered (\item (fmt "{}{}" (sub 100000 (json_get item ["score"])) (json_get item ["name"])))))
(set indexed (enumerate sorted))

  (println (fmt "{}. {} ({}): {}" (add (first pair) 1) (json_get (last pair) ["name"]) (json_get (last pair) ["dept"]) (json_get (last pair) ["score"]))))
(println "---")
(set depts (map_arr sorted (\item (json_get item ["dept"]))))
(set counts (counter depts))
(set dept_entries (sort_by (entries counts) (\pair (first pair))))
(for-each pair dept_entries
  (println (fmt "{}: {}" (first pair) (last pair))))

argv[1] is integer capacity; remaining args are 'value:weight' pairs; solve 0/1 knapsack via DP and print 'Max value: V' where V is the maximum total value with weight <= capacity.

(set cap #0)
(set total (argv_count))
(set vals [])
(set wts [])
(for i 1 total
  (set parts (split (arg_str i) ":"))
  (push vals (int (array_get parts 0)))
  (push wts (int (array_get parts 1))))
(set dp [])
(for i 0 (add cap 1)
  (push dp 0))
(set n (sub total 1))
(for i 0 n
  (set v (array_get vals i))
  (set wt (array_get wts i))
  (set w cap)
  (while (ge w wt)
    (array_set dp w (max (array_get dp w) (add (array_get dp (sub w wt)) v)))
    (set w (sub w 1))))
(println (fmt "Max value: {}" (array_get dp cap)))

(fn parse_config config string -> map
  (set lines array (split config "\n"))
  (set result map {})
  (for-each line string lines
    (if (and (not (eq line "")) (not (string_starts_with line "#")))
      (set parts array (split line "="))
      (set key string (array_get parts 0))
      (set value string (array_get parts 1))
      (set result (+ result {key value}))))
  (ret result))

(fn get_keys_with_value config_map map val string -> array

  (for-each k string (map_keys config_map)
    (if (eq (get config_map k) val)
      (push keys k)))
  (ret (sort keys)))

(for-each key string matching_keys
  (println key))

Each argument is 'A:B'; compute the Levenshtein edit distance (insert/delete/substitute, all cost 1) between A and B and print 'A -> B: N'.

(fn lev a string b string -> int
  (set m (string_length a))
  (set n (string_length b))
  (set w (add n 1))
  (set sz (mul (add m 1) w))
  (set dp [])
  (for k 0 sz (push dp 0))
  (for j 0 (add n 1)
    (array_set dp j j))
  (for i 0 (add m 1)
    (array_set dp (mul i w) i))
  (for i 1 (add m 1)
    (for j 1 (add n 1)
      (set ca (string_get a (sub i 1)))
      (set cb (string_get b (sub j 1)))
      (set idx (add (mul i w) j))
      (set up (array_get dp (sub idx w)))
      (set left (array_get dp (sub idx 1)))
      (set diag (array_get dp (sub (sub idx w) 1)))
      (if (eq ca cb)
        (array_set dp idx diag)
        (else
          (array_set dp idx (add 1 (min (min up left) diag)))))))
  (ret (array_get dp (sub sz 1))))

(set nargs (argv_count))
(for i 0 nargs
  (set arg (arg_str i))
  (set parts (split arg ":"))
  (set a (array_get parts 0))
  (set b (array_get parts 1))
  (println (fmt "{} -> {}: {}" a b (lev a b))))

Compute the Levenshtein edit distance between the two CLI string args using full dynamic programming and print the integer result.

(set a $0)
(set b $1)
(set m (len a))
(set n (len b))
(set ac (chars a))
(set bc (chars b))
(set dp [])
(for i 0 (add m 1)
  (set row [])
  (for j 0 (add n 1)
    (push row 0))
  (push dp row))
(for i 0 (add m 1)
  (array_set (array_get dp i) 0 i))
(for j 0 (add n 1)
  (array_set (array_get dp 0) j j))
(for i 1 (add m 1)
  (for j 1 (add n 1)
    (set cost 1)
    (if (eq (array_get ac (sub i 1)) (array_get bc (sub j 1)))
      (set cost 0))
    (set d1 (add (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) j) 1))
    (set d2 (add (array_get (array_get dp i) (sub j 1)) 1))
    (set d3 (add (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) (sub j 1)) cost))
    (array_set (array_get dp i) j (min (min d1 d2) d3))))
(println (array_get (array_get dp m) n))

Compute the Levenshtein edit distance (insertions, deletions, substitutions) between the two argument strings using a recursive memoized algorithm, and print the integer result.

(fn lev a string b string i int j int memo map -> int
  (set key (fmt "{},{}" i j))
  (if (map_has memo key) (ret (map_get memo key)))
  (cond
    ((eq i 0)
      (map_set memo key j)
      (ret j))
    ((eq j 0)
      (map_set memo key i)
      (ret i)))
  (set ca (string_get a (sub i 1)))
  (set cb (string_get b (sub j 1)))
  (set cost 1)
  (if (eq ca cb) (set cost 0))
  (set d1 (add (lev a b (sub i 1) j memo) 1))
  (set d2 (add (lev a b i (sub j 1) memo) 1))
  (set d3 (add (lev a b (sub i 1) (sub j 1) memo) cost))
  (set result (min d1 (min d2 d3)))
  (map_set memo key result)
  (ret result))

(set a $0)
(set b $1)
(set memo {})
(println (lev a b (len a) (len b) memo))

Compute and print the longest common subsequence of 'ABCBDAB' and 'BDCABA', breaking ties by preferring to move up in the DP table first (dp[i-1][j] >= dp[i][j-1]).

(set s1 $0)
(set s2 $1)
(set m (len s1))
(set n (len s2))

(set dp [])
(for i 0 (add m 1)
  (set row [])
  (for j 0 (add n 1)
    (push row 0))
  (push dp row))

(for i 1 (add m 1)
  (for j 1 (add n 1)
    (set ci (string_get s1 (sub i 1)))
    (set cj (string_get s2 (sub j 1)))
    (if (eq ci cj)
      (set v (add (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) (sub j 1)) 1))
      (else
        (set up (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) j))
        (set le (array_get (array_get dp i) (sub j 1)))
        (set v (max up le))))
    (array_set (array_get dp i) j v)))

(set result "")
(set i m)
(set j n)
(while (and (gt i 0) (gt j 0))
  (set ci (string_get s1 (sub i 1)))
  (set cj (string_get s2 (sub j 1)))
  (if (eq ci cj)
    (set result (string_concat (char_from_code ci) result))
    (set i (sub i 1))
    (set j (sub j 1))
    (else
      (set up (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) j))
      (set le (array_get (array_get dp i) (sub j 1)))
      (if (ge up le)
        (set i (sub i 1))
        (else (set j (sub j 1)))))))

(println result)

From a Markdown file-content string, extract all section headers recording H-level and text in document order and all hyperlinks recording display text and URL in document order, printing each group under a labeled header.

(set md string $0)
(set lines array (split md "\n"))

(set headers array [])
(for-each line string lines
  (if (string_starts_with line "#")
    (set level int 0)
    (set llen int (len line))
    (set hi int 0)
    (while (and (lt hi llen) (eq (string_get line hi) 35))
      (set level (add level 1))
      (set hi (add hi 1)))
    (if (and (le level 6) (and (lt hi llen) (eq (string_get line hi) 32)))
      (set htext string (trim (string_slice line (add hi 1) (sub llen (add hi 1)))))
      (push headers [level htext]))))

(set links array [])
(set mlen int (len md))
(set i int 0)
(while (lt i mlen)
  (if (eq (string_get md i) 91)
    (set j int (add i 1))
    (while (and (lt j mlen) (ne (string_get md j) 93))
      (set j (add j 1)))
    (if (and (lt (add j 1) mlen) (eq (string_get md (add j 1)) 40))
      (set k int (add j 2))
      (while (and (lt k mlen) (ne (string_get md k) 41))
        (set k (add k 1)))
      (if (lt k mlen)
        (set ltext string (string_slice md (add i 1) (sub j (add i 1))))
        (set lurl string (string_slice md (add j 2) (sub k (add j 2))))
        (push links [ltext lurl])
        (set i k))))
  (set i (add i 1)))

(println "Headers:")
(for-each h headers
  (println (fmt "  H{}: {}" (array_get h 0) (array_get h 1))))
(println "Links:")
(for-each lk links
  (println (fmt "  {} -> {}" (array_get lk 0) (array_get lk 1))))

Given a Markdown document blob as the first arg and a max header level (1-3) as the second arg, print every header at that level or higher priority prefixed by '=' for h1, '-' for h2, '~' for h3, then a final 'headers: N' count line.

(fn line_level line string -> int
  (cond
    ((string_starts_with line "### ") (ret 3))
    ((string_starts_with line "## ") (ret 2))
    ((string_starts_with line "# ") (ret 1))
    (true (ret 0))))

(fn header_prefix lv int -> string
  (cond
    ((eq lv 1) (ret "= "))
    ((eq lv 2) (ret "- "))
    (true (ret "~ "))))

(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(set max_level #1)
(set headers (filter lines (\line (and (gt (line_level line) 0) (le (line_level line) max_level)))))
(for-each hdr headers
  (set lv (line_level hdr))
  (println (join [(header_prefix lv) (join (slice (chars hdr) (+ lv 1)) "")] "")))
(println (join ["headers: " (str (len headers))] ""))

Given a markdown document blob containing ATX headers (lines starting with one or more # characters), generate a hierarchical numbered table of contents using two-space indentation per heading level.

(fn count_hash s string -> int
  (cond
    ((string_starts_with s "######") (ret 6))
    ((string_starts_with s "#####") (ret 5))
    ((string_starts_with s "####") (ret 4))
    ((string_starts_with s "###") (ret 3))
    ((string_starts_with s "##") (ret 2))
    (true (ret 1))))

(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(set headers (filter lines (\l (string_starts_with l "#"))))
(set c1 0)
(set c2 0)
(set c3 0)
(set c4 0)
(set c5 0)
(set c6 0)

(for-each line headers
  (set level (count_hash line))
  (set text (slice line (+ level 1) (len line)))
  (set c1 (cond ((= level 1) (+ c1 1)) (true c1)))
  (set c2 (cond ((= level 2) (+ c2 1)) ((< level 2) 0) (true c2)))
  (set c3 (cond ((= level 3) (+ c3 1)) ((< level 3) 0) (true c3)))
  (set c4 (cond ((= level 4) (+ c4 1)) ((< level 4) 0) (true c4)))
  (set c5 (cond ((= level 5) (+ c5 1)) ((< level 5) 0) (true c5)))
  (set c6 (cond ((= level 6) (+ c6 1)) ((< level 6) 0) (true c6)))
  (set numstr (cond
    ((= level 1) (str c1))
    ((= level 2) (fmt "{}.{}" c1 c2))
    ((= level 3) (fmt "{}.{}.{}" c1 c2 c3))
    ((= level 4) (fmt "{}.{}.{}.{}" c1 c2 c3 c4))
    ((= level 5) (fmt "{}.{}.{}.{}.{}" c1 c2 c3 c4 c5))
    (true (fmt "{}.{}.{}.{}.{}.{}" c1 c2 c3 c4 c5 c6))))
  (set indent (join (map_arr (range 0 (* 2 (- level 1))) (\i " ")) ""))
  (println (fmt "{}{}. {}" indent numstr text)))

Given a grid (rows separated by \n) with 'S' start, 'E' end, '#' wall, '.' open, print the shortest path length from S to E using 4-directional BFS.

(set grid_str $0)
(set rows (split grid_str "\n"))
(set h (len rows))
(set w (len (array_get rows 0)))
(set sr 0)
(set sc 0)
(set er 0)
(set ec 0)
(for r 0 h
  (set row (array_get rows r))
  (for c 0 w
    (set ch (string_slice row c 1))
    (if (eq ch "S") (set sr r) (set sc c) (else))
    (if (eq ch "E") (set er r) (set ec c) (else))))
(set queue [])
(push queue [sr sc 0])
(set visited {})
(set visited (map_set visited (fmt "{},{}" sr sc) true))
(set dirs [[-1 0] [1 0] [0 -1] [0 1]])
(set found -1)
(while (and (gt (len queue) 0) (lt found 0))
  (set cur (array_get queue 0))
  (set queue (array_slice queue 1 (sub (len queue) 1)))
  (set cr (array_get cur 0))
  (set cc (array_get cur 1))
  (set cd (array_get cur 2))
  (if (and (eq cr er) (eq cc ec))
    (set found cd)
    (else
      (for-each dir dirs
        (set nr (add cr (array_get dir 0)))
        (set nc (add cc (array_get dir 1)))
        (if (and (and (ge nr 0) (lt nr h)) (and (ge nc 0) (lt nc w)))
          (set nch (string_slice (array_get rows nr) nc 1))
          (set nkey (fmt "{},{}" nr nc))
          (if (and (ne nch "#") (not (map_has visited nkey)))
            (set visited (map_set visited nkey true))
            (push queue [nr nc (add cd 1)])))))))
(println found)

argv[1]=rows, argv[2]=cols, remaining args are 'r,c' obstacle cells; count paths from (0,0) to (rows-1,cols-1) moving only right or down, avoiding obstacles, and print 'Paths: N'.

(set rows #0)
(set cols #1)
(set total_args (argv_count))

(set obstacles {})
(for i 2 total_args
  (map_set obstacles (arg_str i) true))

(set dp [])
(set total (mul rows cols))
(for i 0 total
  (push dp 0))

(for i 0 rows
  (for j 0 cols
    (set idx (add (mul i cols) j))
    (set key (fmt "{},{}" i j))
    (cond
      ((in key obstacles) (array_set dp idx 0))
      ((and (eq i 0) (eq j 0)) (array_set dp idx 1))
      (true
        (set up 0)
        (set left 0)
        (if (gt i 0)
          (set up (array_get dp (sub idx cols))))
        (if (gt j 0)
          (set left (array_get dp (sub idx 1))))
        (array_set dp idx (add up left))))))

(println (fmt "Paths: {}" (array_get dp (sub total 1))))

(fn is_prime n int -> bool
  (if (lt n 2) (ret false))
  (if (eq n 2) (ret true))
  (if (eq (mod n 2) 0) (ret false))
  (set i 3)
  (while (le (mul i i) n)
    (if (eq (mod n i) 0) (ret false))
    (set i (add i 2)))
  (ret true))

  (set i (sub (len cs) 1))

    (set d (string_to_int (array_get cs i)))

    (set i (sub i 1)))

Convert the integer in argv[1] (0..999) into lowercase English words separated by single spaces with no 'and' connector; print the result.

(set ones ["zero" "one" "two" "three" "four" "five" "six" "seven" "eight" "nine"])
(set teens ["ten" "eleven" "twelve" "thirteen" "fourteen" "fifteen" "sixteen" "seventeen" "eighteen" "nineteen"])
(set tens ["" "" "twenty" "thirty" "forty" "fifty" "sixty" "seventy" "eighty" "ninety"])
(set n #0)
(if (eq n 0)
  (println "zero")
  (else
    (set parts [])
    (set h (div n 100))
    (set r (mod n 100))
    (if (gt h 0)
      (push parts (fmt "{} hundred" (array_get ones h))))
    (if (gt r 0)
      (cond
        ((lt r 10) (push parts (array_get ones r)))
        ((lt r 20) (push parts (array_get teens (sub r 10))))
        (true
          (set t (div r 10))
          (set o (mod r 10))
          (if (eq o 0)
            (push parts (array_get tens t))
            (else
              (push parts (fmt "{} {}" (array_get tens t) (array_get ones o))))))))
    (println (join parts " "))))

Convert an integer 0..999 to lowercase English words, separated by single spaces (e.g. 742 -> 'seven hundred forty two').

(fn words n int -> string
  (set ones ["zero" "one" "two" "three" "four" "five" "six" "seven" "eight" "nine" "ten" "eleven" "twelve" "thirteen" "fourteen" "fifteen" "sixteen" "seventeen" "eighteen" "nineteen"])
  (set tens ["" "" "twenty" "thirty" "forty" "fifty" "sixty" "seventy" "eighty" "ninety"])
  (if (eq n 0) (ret "zero"))
  (set parts [])
  (set h (div n 100))
  (set r (mod n 100))
  (if (gt h 0)
    (push parts (array_get ones h))
    (push parts "hundred"))
  (if (lt r 20)
    (if (gt r 0) (push parts (array_get ones r)))
    (else
      (push parts (array_get tens (div r 10)))
      (set u (mod r 10))
      (if (gt u 0) (push parts (array_get ones u)))))
  (ret (join parts " ")))

(println (words #0))

Given a comma-separated list of integers and a positive integer N, print count, sum, mean (2 decimals), median (2 decimals), and the top N values descending comma-separated, each on a labeled line.

(fn fmt2 x int -> string
  (set whole (div x 100))
  (set frac (mod x 100))
  (set fs (str frac))
  (if (lt frac 10) (set fs (string_concat "0" fs)))
  (ret (fmt "{}.{}" whole fs)))

(set arr (map_arr (split $0 ",") (\x (int x))))
(set n #1)
(set count (len arr))
(set total (sum arr))
(set mean_c (round (div (mul (float total) 100.0) (float count))))
(set sorted (sort (array_copy arr)))
(set mid (div count 2))
(set median_c 0)
(if (eq (mod count 2) 0)
  (set median_c (div (mul (add (array_get sorted (sub mid 1)) (array_get sorted mid)) 100) 2))
  (else (set median_c (mul (array_get sorted mid) 100))))
(set desc (rev (array_copy sorted)))
(set topn (slice desc 0 n))
(println (fmt "count: {}" count))
(println (fmt "sum: {}" total))
(println (fmt "mean: {}" (fmt2 mean_c)))
(println (fmt "median: {}" (fmt2 median_c)))
(println (fmt "top{}: {}" n (join topn ",")))

(fn spell n int -> string
  (set ones ["zero" "one" "two" "three" "four" "five" "six" "seven" "eight" "nine" "ten" "eleven" "twelve" "thirteen" "fourteen" "fifteen" "sixteen" "seventeen" "eighteen" "nineteen"])

(fn say n int -> string
  (set ones ["zero" "one" "two" "three" "four" "five" "six" "seven" "eight" "nine" "ten" "eleven" "twelve" "thirteen" "fourteen" "fifteen" "sixteen" "seventeen" "eighteen" "nineteen"])

Print the prime factorization of N in the form 'p1^e1 * p2^e2 * ...' with primes ascending; omit '^1' for exponent 1.

(fn factor n int -> array
  (set parts [])
  (set m int n)
  (set p int 2)
  (while (le (mul p p) m)
    (set e int 0)
    (while (eq (mod m p) 0)
      (set m (div m p))
      (set e (add e 1)))
    (cond
      ((eq e 1) (push parts (str p)))
      ((gt e 1) (push parts (fmt "{}^{}" p e))))
    (set p (add p 1)))
  (if (gt m 1)
    (push parts (str m)))
  (ret parts))

Using the Sieve of Eratosthenes, print all primes less than or equal to N (arg 1) as a comma-separated list on a single line; for N < 2 print an empty line.

(set n #0)
(if (lt n 2)
  (println "")
  (else
    (set sieve [])
    (for i 0 (add n 1) (push sieve true))
    (array_set sieve 0 false)
    (array_set sieve 1 false)
    (for i 2 (add n 1)

Write a Sigil program that implements rec fib n.

(fn fib n int -> int
  (if (lt n 2) (ret n))
  (set a 0)
  (set b 1)
  (for i 0 (sub n 1)
    (set t (add a b))
    (set a b)
    (set b t))
  (ret b))

(println (fib #0))

Take a config string. Print key:value pairs as right-aligned 'key=value' rows, where the right side is padded so all '=' align.

(set lines array (filter (split $0 "\n") (\line (and (not (eq line "")) (ne (first line) "#")))))
(set klen int 1)
(for-each line string lines
  (set parts array (split line "="))
  (if (gt (len (first parts)) klen) (set klen (len (first parts)))))
(set rows array (map_arr lines (\line
  (set parts array (split line "="))
  (set key string (first parts))
  (set val string (last parts))
  (set ks int (sub klen (len key)))
  (set padding string "")
  (for i 0 ks (set padding (string_concat padding " ")))
  (ret (fmt "{}{}={}" padding key val)))))
(println (join rows "\n"))

  (set c (array_get chars i))

For each Roman numeral argument, compute its integer value (with subtractive notation) and print 'ROMAN = VALUE'.

(fn roman_to_int s string -> int
  (set vals {"I" 1 "V" 5 "X" 10 "L" 50 "C" 100 "D" 500 "M" 1000})
  (set cs (chars s))
  (set n (len cs))
  (set total 0)
  (for i 0 n
    (set v (map_get vals (array_get cs i)))
    (set nv 0)
    (if (lt i (sub n 1))
      (set nv (map_get vals (array_get cs (add i 1)))))
    (if (lt v nv)
      (set total (sub total v))
      (else (set total (add total v)))))
  (ret total))

(for-each s (argv)
  (println (fmt "{} = {}" s (roman_to_int s))))

Evaluate a space-separated reverse-Polish expression using integer arithmetic (floor division for '/') and print the final stack top.

(set expr $0)
(set tokens (split expr " "))
(set stack [])
(for-each tok tokens
  (cond
    ((eq tok "+") (set b (array_pop stack)) (set a (array_pop stack)) (push stack (add a b)))
    ((eq tok "-") (set b (array_pop stack)) (set a (array_pop stack)) (push stack (sub a b)))
    ((eq tok "*") (set b (array_pop stack)) (set a (array_pop stack)) (push stack (mul a b)))
    ((eq tok "/") (set b (array_pop stack)) (set a (array_pop stack)) (push stack (div a b)))
    (true (push stack (int tok)))))
(println (last stack))
