Evaluates Reverse Polish Notation expressions from input.

(fn eval_rpn expr string -> string
  (set toks (split expr " "))
  (set stack [])
  (set used_div false)
  (for-each tok toks
    (cond
      ((eq tok "+")
        (set b (array_pop stack))
        (set a (array_pop stack))
        (array_push stack (add a b)))
      ((eq tok "-")
        (set b (array_pop stack))
        (set a (array_pop stack))
        (array_push stack (sub a b)))
      ((eq tok "*")
        (set b (array_pop stack))
        (set a (array_pop stack))
        (array_push stack (mul a b)))
      ((eq tok "/")
        (set b (array_pop stack))
        (set a (array_pop stack))
        (set used_div true)
        (array_push stack (div a b)))
      (true
        (array_push stack (float tok)))))
  (set result (array_pop stack))
  (if used_div
    (set s (str result))
    (if (string_ends_with s ".")
      (ret (string_concat s "0"))
      (else (ret s))))
  (ret (str (int result))))

(for i 0 (argv_count)
  (println (eval_rpn (arg_str i))))

    ((eq op "SUB")

      (set a (array_pop stack))
      (push stack (sub a b)))
    ((eq op "MUL")
      (set b (array_pop stack))
      (set a (array_pop stack))
      (push stack (mul a b)))
    ((eq op "DUP") (push stack (last stack)))
    ((eq op "PRINT") (println (last stack)))))

Given comma-separated allowed transitions like 'a->b,b->c' and a comma-separated state sequence, print 'valid' if every consecutive pair is an allowed transition, else 'invalid at step N' for the 1-indexed first bad transition.

(set trans_list (split $0 ","))
(set states (split $1 ","))
(set n (len states))
(set indices (range 0 (- n 1)))
(set bad (filter indices (\i (not (in (join [(get_or states i "") (get_or states (+ i 1) "")] "->") trans_list)))))
(cond
  ((eq 0 (len bad)) (println "valid"))
  (true (println (join ["invalid at step " (str (+ (first bad) 1))] ""))))

Take a string in arg0 and print the length of the longest run of identical adjacent characters.

(set input $0)
(set max_run 1)
(set current_run 1)
(set prev_char "")
(for-each ch (chars input)
  (if (eq ch prev_char)
    (set current_run (+ current_run 1))
    (else
      (set max_run (max max_run current_run))
      (set current_run 1)))
  (set prev_char ch))
(set max_run (max max_run current_run))
(println max_run)

Take two strings in arg0 and arg1. Print the Jaccard similarity of their character sets (size of intersection divided by size of union), as a float with 3 decimal places. If both empty, print '0.000'.

(set sa {})
(for-each c (chars $0)
  (set sa (map_set sa c true)))
(set sb {})
(for-each c (chars $1)
  (set sb (map_set sb c true)))
(set inter 0)
(for-each k (map_keys sa)
  (if (map_has sb k) (set inter (add inter 1))))
(set un (sub (add (map_length sa) (map_length sb)) inter))
(if (eq un 0)
  (println "0.000")
  (else
    (set ratio (div (cast_int_float inter) (cast_int_float un)))
    (set n (round (mul ratio 1000.0)))
    (set whole (div n 1000))
    (set frac (mod n 1000))
    (set fs (string_from_int frac))
    (while (lt (len fs) 3)
      (set fs (string_concat "0" fs)))
    (println (fmt "{}.{}" whole fs))))

Validate the NxN sudoku grid in argv[1] (rows separated by '|', cells are digits or '.'); print 'valid' if no row, column, or sqrt(N)-sized box has duplicate non-empty values, else 'invalid'.

(set raw $0)
(set rows (split raw "|"))
(set n (len rows))
(set sq (cast_float_int (sqrt (cast_int_float n))))

(set grid [])
(for i 0 n
  (set grid (push grid (chars (array_get rows i)))))

(set valid true)

(for i 0 n
  (set seen_r {})
  (set seen_c {})
  (for j 0 n
    (set r (array_get (array_get grid i) j))
    (set c (array_get (array_get grid j) i))
    (if (ne r ".")
      (if (map_has seen_r r)
        (set valid false)
        (else (set seen_r (map_set seen_r r true)))))
    (if (ne c ".")
      (if (map_has seen_c c)
        (set valid false)
        (else (set seen_c (map_set seen_c c true)))))))

(for bi 0 sq
  (for bj 0 sq
    (set seen_b {})
    (for r 0 sq
      (for c 0 sq
        (set ri (add (mul bi sq) r))
        (set ci (add (mul bj sq) c))
        (set ch (array_get (array_get grid ri) ci))
        (if (ne ch ".")
          (if (map_has seen_b ch)
            (set valid false)
            (else (set seen_b (map_set seen_b ch true)))))))))

(if valid
  (println "valid")
  (else (println "invalid")))

Tokenize an arithmetic expression into one token per line: 'NUM <digits>' for runs of digits, 'OP <c>' for + - * /, ignoring spaces, and 'UNK <c>' for anything else.

(set s $0)
(set n (len s))
(set i 0)
(while (lt i n)
  (set c (string_get s i))
  (cond
    ((is_digit c)
      (set j i)
      (while (and (lt j n) (is_digit (string_get s j)))
        (set j (add j 1)))
      (println (fmt "NUM {}" (string_slice s i (sub j i))))
      (set i j))
    ((eq c 32)
      (set i (add i 1)))
    ((in (char_from_code c) "+-*/")
      (println (fmt "OP {}" (char_from_code c)))
      (set i (add i 1)))
    (true
      (println (fmt "UNK {}" (char_from_code c)))
      (set i (add i 1)))))

Tokenize the arithmetic expression '3+42*(2-1)' into NUM / OP / LPAREN / RPAREN tokens and print one 'TYPE:value' per line in order.

(set s $0)
(set n (len s))
(set i 0)
(while (lt i n)
  (set ch (string_slice s i 1))
  (cond
    ((is_digit ch)
      (set start i)
      (while (and (lt i n) (is_digit (string_slice s i 1)))
        (set i (add i 1)))
      (println (fmt "NUM:{}" (string_slice s start (sub i start)))))
    ((eq ch "(")
      (println "LPAREN:(")
      (set i (add i 1)))
    ((eq ch ")")
      (println "RPAREN:)")
      (set i (add i 1)))
    (true
      (println (fmt "OP:{}" ch))
      (set i (add i 1)))))

Tokenize argv[1] into NAME(identifier), NUM(integer), and OP(single non-alnum non-space char) tokens; print one token per line in source order.

(set s $0)
(set arr (chars s))
(set n (len arr))
(set i 0)
(while (lt i n)
  (set c (array_get arr i))
  (cond
    ((is_whitespace c) (set i (add i 1)))
    ((is_digit c)
      (set tok "")
      (while (and (lt i n) (is_digit (array_get arr i)))
        (set tok (string_concat tok (array_get arr i)))
        (set i (add i 1)))
      (println (fmt "NUM({})" tok)))
    ((is_alpha c)
      (set tok "")
      (while (and (lt i n) (is_alpha (array_get arr i)))
        (set tok (string_concat tok (array_get arr i)))
        (set i (add i 1)))
      (println (fmt "NAME({})" tok)))
    (true
      (println (fmt "OP({})" c))
      (set i (add i 1)))))

Tokenize a simple expression into IDENT(name), NUM(value), OP(+|-|*|/|=), LPAREN, RPAREN tokens (whitespace separates), printing one token per line.

(set src $0)
(set n (len src))
(set i 0)
(while (lt i n)
  (set c (string_get src i))
  (cond
    ((is_whitespace c) (set i (add i 1)))
    ((eq c 40) (println "LPAREN") (set i (add i 1)))
    ((eq c 41) (println "RPAREN") (set i (add i 1)))
    ((in c [43 45 42 47 61])
       (println (fmt "OP({})" (char_from_code c)))
       (set i (add i 1)))
    ((is_digit c)
       (set j i)
       (while (and (lt j n) (is_digit (string_get src j)))
         (set j (add j 1)))
       (println (fmt "NUM({})" (string_slice src i (sub j i))))
       (set i j))
    ((is_alpha c)
       (set j i)
       (while (and (lt j n) (is_alpha (string_get src j)))
         (set j (add j 1)))
       (println (fmt "IDENT({})" (string_slice src i (sub j i))))
       (set i j))
    (true (set i (add i 1)))))

    (true
      (println (fmt "OP:{}" (char_from_code c)))
      (set i (add i 1)))))

(set tuples [])

Parse 'name:dep1,dep2' lines (deps must precede name) and print a stable topological order (Kahn's algorithm with alphabetical tie-breaks) as space-separated names.

(set input $0)
(set lines (split input "\n"))
(set deps {})
(set indeg {})
(set nodes [])

(for-each line lines
  (set parts (split line ":"))
  (set name (array_get parts 0))
  (set dep_str "")
  (if (> (len parts) 1)
    (set dep_str (array_get parts 1)))
  (if (not (in name nodes))
    (set nodes (push nodes name)))
  (set dlist [])
  (if (> (len dep_str) 0)
    (set dlist (split dep_str ",")))
  (set deps (map_set deps name dlist))
  (set indeg (map_set indeg name (len dlist))))

(set result [])
(set processed {})

(while (< (len result) (len nodes))
  (set ready [])
  (for-each n nodes
    (if (and (not (in n processed)) (eq (map_get indeg n) 0))
      (set ready (push ready n))))
  (set ready (sort ready))
  (set pick (first ready))
  (set result (push result pick))
  (set processed (map_set processed pick true))
  (for-each n nodes
    (if (in pick (map_get deps n))
      (set indeg (map_set indeg n (- (map_get indeg n) 1))))))

(println (join result " "))

Given comma-separated directed edges of form 'a->b', print a topological ordering of nodes space-separated, breaking ties alphabetically (Kahn's algorithm with a min-heap).

(set edges (split $0 ","))
(set adj {})
(set indeg {})
(set nodes {})

(for-each e edges
  (set parts (split e "->"))
  (set a (array_get parts 0))
  (set b (array_get parts 1))
  (map_set nodes a true)
  (map_set nodes b true)
  (if (not (map_has adj a)) (map_set adj a []))
  (if (not (map_has indeg a)) (map_set indeg a 0))
  (if (not (map_has indeg b)) (map_set indeg b 0))
  (set ns (map_get adj a))
  (push ns b)
  (map_set adj a ns)
  (map_set indeg b (add (map_get indeg b) 1)))

(set ready [])
(for-each n (map_keys nodes)
  (if (eq (map_get indeg n) 0) (push ready n)))

(set result [])
(while (gt (len ready) 0)
  (sort ready)
  (set node (array_get ready 0))
  (set ready (array_remove ready 0))
  (push result node)
  (if (map_has adj node)
    (for-each nbr (map_get adj node)
      (set d (sub (map_get indeg nbr) 1))
      (map_set indeg nbr d)
      (if (eq d 0) (push ready nbr)))))

(println (join result " "))

Given dependencies in the form 'name:dep1,dep2;name2:dep;...' (deps must come before dependents), print a topological order as 'a -> b -> c' with ties broken alphabetically.

(set raw $0)
(set parts (split raw ";"))

(set indeg {})
(set dependents {})
(set all_nodes [])

(for-each part parts
  (if (gt (len part) 0)
    (set kv (split part ":"))
    (set name (array_get kv 0))
    (push all_nodes name)
    (map_set indeg name 0)
    (set dep_str "")
    (if (gt (len kv) 1)
      (set dep_str (array_get kv 1)))
    (if (gt (len dep_str) 0)
      (set dlist (split dep_str ","))
      (for-each d dlist
        (map_set indeg name (add (map_get indeg name) 1))
        (set cur [])
        (if (map_has dependents d)
          (set cur (map_get dependents d)))
        (push cur name)
        (map_set dependents d cur)))))

(set ready [])
(for-each n all_nodes
  (if (eq (map_get indeg n) 0)
    (push ready n)))
(sort ready)

(set result [])
(while (gt (len ready) 0)
  (set node (array_get ready 0))
  (set ready (array_slice ready 1 (sub (len ready) 1)))
  (push result node)
  (if (map_has dependents node)
    (set deps_of (map_get dependents node))
    (for-each m deps_of
      (map_set indeg m (sub (map_get indeg m) 1))
      (if (eq (map_get indeg m) 0)
        (push ready m)))
    (sort ready)))

(println (join result " -> "))

Parse a dependency graph from argv[1] formatted as 'node:dep1,dep2;node:dep' and print a Kahn-style topological sort with lexicographic tie-breaking, space-separated on one line.

(set graph_str string $0)
(set segs array (split graph_str ";"))
(set in_deg map {})
(set dependents map {})
(set all_nodes array [])

(for-each seg string segs
  (set parts array (split seg ":"))
  (set node string (array_get parts 0))
  (if (not (in node all_nodes))
    (set all_nodes (push all_nodes node))
    (set dependents (map_set dependents node []))
    (set in_deg (map_set in_deg node 0)))
  (set deps_str string (get_or parts 1 ""))
  (if (gt (len deps_str) 0)
    (set deps array (split deps_str ","))
    (for-each dep string deps
      (if (not (in dep all_nodes))
        (set all_nodes (push all_nodes dep))
        (set dependents (map_set dependents dep []))
        (set in_deg (map_set in_deg dep 0)))
      (set in_deg (map_set in_deg node (add (map_get in_deg node) 1)))
      (set cur_dep array (map_get dependents dep))
      (set cur_dep (push cur_dep node))
      (set dependents (map_set dependents dep cur_dep)))))

(set result array [])
(while (gt (len all_nodes) 0)
  (set sorted array (sort (array_copy all_nodes)))
  (set picked string "")
  (set found bool false)
  (for-each n string sorted
    (if (and (not found) (eq (map_get in_deg n) 0))
      (set picked n)
      (set found true)))
  (if (not found)
    (set all_nodes []))
  (if found
    (set result (push result picked))
    (set new_nodes array [])
    (for-each n string all_nodes
      (if (not (eq n picked))
        (set new_nodes (push new_nodes n))))
    (set all_nodes new_nodes)
    (set deps_of_picked array (map_get dependents picked))
    (for-each d string deps_of_picked
      (set in_deg (map_set in_deg d (sub (map_get in_deg d) 1))))))

(println (join result " "))

Recursively solve Tower of Hanoi for N (arg1) disks moving from peg arg2 to peg arg3 using peg arg4, printing each move as 'k: SRC->DST' with 1-based step numbers.

(fn hanoi n int src string dst string via string k int -> int
  (if (eq n 0) (ret k))
  (set k (hanoi (sub n 1) src via dst k))
  (println (fmt "{}: {}->{}" k src dst))
  (ret (hanoi (sub n 1) via dst src (add k 1))))

(hanoi #0 $1 $2 $3 1)

Parse arg1 as comma-separated 'parent:child' pairs, build a tree, and print it with two-space indentation per depth level (preorder, children in input order).

(set pairs (split $0 ","))
(set children {})
(set all_parents [])
(set all_children [])

(for-each pair pairs
  (set parts (split pair ":"))
  (set p (first parts))
  (set c (last parts))
  (push all_parents p)
  (push all_children c)
  (set kids (get_or children p []))
  (push kids c)
  (map_set children p kids))

(set root "")
(for-each q all_parents
  (if (not (in q all_children))
    (set root q)))

(fn dfs node string depth int kids map -> int
  (set indent "")
  (for i 0 depth
    (set indent (string_concat indent "  ")))
  (println (string_concat indent node))
  (if (map_has kids node)
    (for-each child (map_get kids node)
      (dfs child (add depth 1) kids)))
  (ret 0))

(dfs root 0 children)

Each argument is a 'parent,child' edge; print the resulting forest depth-first, indenting each node by 2 spaces per depth level, preserving child insertion order, with roots (nodes never appearing as a child) printed in sorted order.

(fn walk node string kids_map map depth int -> int
  (set indent "")
  (for i 0 (mul depth 2)
    (set indent (string_concat indent " ")))
  (println (string_concat indent node))
  (if (map_has kids_map node)
    (for-each k (map_get kids_map node)
      (walk k kids_map (add depth 1))))
  (ret 0))

(set kids_map {})
(set all_children {})
(set all_nodes {})
(for-each edge (argv)
  (set parts (split edge ","))
  (set p (array_get parts 0))
  (set c (array_get parts 1))
  (set all_nodes (map_set all_nodes p true))
  (set all_nodes (map_set all_nodes c true))
  (set all_children (map_set all_children c true))
  (if (not (map_has kids_map p))
    (set kids_map (map_set kids_map p [])))
  (set ks (map_get kids_map p))
  (set ks (push ks c))
  (set kids_map (map_set kids_map p ks)))

(set roots [])
(for-each n (map_keys all_nodes)
  (if (not (map_has all_children n))
    (set roots (push roots n))))
(set roots (sort roots))

(for-each r roots
  (walk r kids_map 0))

Build a tree from comma-separated slash-paths (e.g. 'a/b,a/c,d/e/f') and print it depth-first preserving insertion order, indenting each level by two spaces.

(set paths (split $0 ","))
(set seen {})
(for-each path paths
  (set segs (split path "/"))
  (for-each i (range 0 (len segs))
    (set prefix (slice segs 0 (+ i 1)))
    (set key (join prefix "/"))
    (set seg (last prefix))
    (set indent_str (join (map_arr (range 0 (* i 2)) (\_ " ")) ""))
    (set was_seen (in key seen))
    (set seen (json_set seen [key] true))
    (if (eq was_seen false)
      (println (join [indent_str seg] "")))))

Simplify a Unix-style absolute path by resolving '.' and '..' components and removing empty segments, printing the canonical path.

(set parts (filter (split $0 "/") (\p (and (ne p "") (ne p ".")))))
(set stack [])
(for-each p parts
  (if (eq p "..")
    (if (gt (len stack) 0) (array_pop stack))
    (else (push stack p))))
(println (string_concat "/" (join stack "/")))

Take a sentence. Print it with the first letter of each whitespace-separated word uppercased, rest lowercased.

(set s string $0)
(set words array (split s " "))
(set result string "")
(for-each w string words
  (set first char (first (chars w)))
  (set rest string (lower (slice w 1)))
  (set uc_first string (upper first))
  (if (eq result "") (set result (fmt "{}{}" uc_first rest))
    (else (set result (fmt "{} {}{}" result uc_first rest)))))
(println (fmt "{}\n" result))

Given a newline-delimited list of URLs as the first arg and a component name ('scheme', 'host', 'path', or 'query') as the second arg, extract that component from every URL, deduplicate, sort alphabetically, print each value, then print 'unique: N'.

(fn extract url string comp string -> string
  (set parts (split url "://"))
  (set scheme_val (first parts))
  (set rest (last parts))
  (set no_query (first (split rest "?")))
  (set host_val (first (split no_query "/")))
  (set path_start (len host_val))
  (set path_val (slice no_query path_start (len no_query)))
  (set qparts (split rest "?"))
  (set query_val (if (gt (len qparts) 1) (last qparts) ""))
  (cond
    ((eq comp "scheme") (ret scheme_val))
    ((eq comp "host") (ret host_val))
    ((eq comp "path") (ret path_val))
    (true (ret query_val))))

(set urls (filter (split $0 "\n") (\u (gt (len u) 0))))
(set components (map_arr urls (\u (extract u $1))))
(set result (uniq (sort components)))
(for-each v result
  (println v))
(println (fmt "unique: {}" (len result)))

Parse a URL string into scheme, host, path, and query parameters, printing each on its own labeled line and listing query params sorted alphabetically under a params: header with two-space indentation

(set url string $0)
(set scheme string (first (split url "://")))
(set rest string (last (split url "://")))
(set host_path_query array (split rest "/"))
(set host string (first host_path_query))
(set path_query string (join (slice host_path_query 1) "/"))
(set path string "")
(set query string "")
(if (in "?" path_query)
    (set path string (first (split path_query "?"))))
(if (in "?" path_query)
    (set query string (last (split path_query "?"))))
(set params array [])
(if (not (eq query ""))
    (set params (split query "&")))
(set sorted_params array (sort params))
(println (fmt "scheme: {}" scheme))
(println (fmt "host: {}" host))
(println (fmt "path: /{}" path))
(println "params:")
(for-each param string sorted_params
    (println (fmt "  {}" param)))

Take two strings of the same length. Print the Levenshtein edit distance.

(set a string $0)
(set b string $1)
(set m int (len a))
(set n int (len b))
(set dp [])
(for i 0 (add m 1)
  (set row [])
  (for j 0 (add n 1)
    (push row 0))
  (push dp row))
(for i 0 (add m 1)
  (array_set (array_get dp i) 0 i))
(for j 0 (add n 1)
  (array_set (array_get dp 0) j j))
(for i 1 (add m 1)
  (for j 1 (add n 1)
    (set cost int 1)
    (if (eq (string_get a (sub i 1)) (string_get b (sub j 1)))
      (set cost 0))
    (set d1 int (add (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) j) 1))
    (set d2 int (add (array_get (array_get dp i) (sub j 1)) 1))
    (set d3 int (add (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) (sub j 1)) cost))
    (array_set (array_get dp i) j (min (min d1 d2) d3))))
(println (array_get (array_get dp m) n))

Take two strings. If their Levenshtein edit distance is exactly 1, print yes; otherwise print no.

(set s1 string $0)
(set s2 string $1)
(set m int (len s1))
(set n int (len s2))
(set dp array [])
(for i 0 (add m 1)
  (set row array [])
  (for j 0 (add n 1)
    (push row 0))
  (push dp row))

(for i 0 (add m 1)
  (array_set (array_get dp i) 0 i))

(for j 0 (add n 1)
  (array_set (array_get dp 0) j j))

(for i 1 (add m 1)
  (for j 1 (add n 1)
    (set ca int (string_get s1 (sub i 1)))
    (set cb int (string_get s2 (sub j 1)))
    (if (eq ca cb)
      (array_set (array_get dp i) j (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) (sub j 1)))
      (else
        (set up int (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) j))
        (set left int (array_get (array_get dp i) (sub j 1)))
        (set diag int (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) (sub j 1)))
        (array_set (array_get dp i) j (add 1 (min (min up left) diag)))))))

(set d int (array_get (array_get dp m) n))
(if (eq d 1)
  (println "yes")
  (else
    (println "no")))

Take two strings in arg0 and arg1. Print the Levenshtein edit distance.

(set a $0)
(set b $1)
(set m (len a))
(set n (len b))
(set dp [])
(for i 0 (add m 1)
  (set row [])
  (for j 0 (add n 1)
    (push row 0))
  (push dp row))
(for i 0 (add m 1)
  (array_set (array_get dp i) 0 i))
(for j 0 (add n 1)
  (array_set (array_get dp 0) j j))
(for i 1 (add m 1)
  (for j 1 (add n 1)
    (set cost 1)
    (if (eq (string_get a (sub i 1)) (string_get b (sub j 1)))
      (set cost 0))
    (set d1 (add (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) j) 1))
    (set d2 (add (array_get (array_get dp i) (sub j 1)) 1))
    (set d3 (add (array_get (array_get dp (sub i 1)) (sub j 1)) cost))
    (array_set (array_get dp i) j (min (min d1 d2) d3))))
(println (array_get (array_get dp m) n))

Print yes if arg1 is a valid YYYY-MM-DD date, else no.

(fn valid s string -> bool
  (if (ne (len s) 10) (ret false))
  (if (ne (string_get s 4) 45) (ret false))
  (if (ne (string_get s 7) 45) (ret false))
  (for i 0 10
    (if (and (ne i 4) (ne i 7))
      (if (not (is_digit (string_get s i))) (ret false))))
  (set y (string_to_int (string_slice s 0 4)))
  (set m (string_to_int (string_slice s 5 2)))
  (set d (string_to_int (string_slice s 8 2)))
  (if (or (lt m 1) (gt m 12)) (ret false))
  (if (lt d 1) (ret false))
  (set max_d 31)
  (if (or (eq m 4) (or (eq m 6) (or (eq m 9) (eq m 11))))
    (set max_d 30))
  (if (eq m 2)
    (set leap false)
    (if (eq (mod y 4) 0) (set leap true))
    (if (eq (mod y 100) 0) (set leap false))
    (if (eq (mod y 400) 0) (set leap true))
    (if leap (set max_d 29) (else (set max_d 28))))
  (if (gt d max_d) (ret false))
  (ret true))

(try
  (if (valid $0) (println "yes") (else (println "no")))
  (catch e string (println "no")))

Counts the occurrences of each word in a given text and outputs them sorted by frequency descending then alphabetically.

(set vocab ["the" "quick" "brown" "fox" "jumps" "over" "lazy" "grey" "dog"])
(set text $0)
(set words (split text " "))
(set counts {})
(for-each w vocab
  (set counts (map_set counts w 0)))
(for-each w words
  (if (map_has counts w)
    (set counts (map_set counts w (add (map_get counts w) 1)))))
(set max_count 0)

  (set v (map_get counts w))
  (if (gt v max_count) (set max_count v)))
(for i 0 (add max_count 1)
  (set count (sub max_count i))
  (set group [])
  (for-each w vocab
    (if (eq (map_get counts w) count)
      (set group (push group w))))
  (set group (sort group))
  (for-each k group
    (println (fmt "{} {}" k count))))

  (set ec (array_get e 1))

Given an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) as the first CLI argument and a non-negative integer N as the second, print the ISO date that lies exactly N business days (Monday-Friday) after the start date.

(fn is_leap y int -> bool
  (or (and (eq (mod y 4) 0) (ne (mod y 100) 0)) (eq (mod y 400) 0)))

(fn dim y int m int -> int
  (cond
    ((eq m 2)
      (if (is_leap y) (ret 29))
      (ret 28))
    ((or (eq m 4) (or (eq m 6) (or (eq m 9) (eq m 11))))
      (ret 30))
    (true (ret 31))))

(fn dow y int m int d int -> int
  (set yy y)
  (set mm m)
  (if (lt mm 3) (set mm (add mm 12)) (set yy (sub yy 1)) (else))
  (set k (mod yy 100))
  (set j (div yy 100))
  (set h (add d (add (div (mul 13 (add mm 1)) 5) (add k (add (div k 4) (sub (div j 4) (mul 2 j)))))))
  (set h (mod (add (mod h 7) 7) 7))
  (ret h))

(set parts (split $0 "-"))
(set y (int (array_get parts 0)))
(set m (int (array_get parts 1)))
(set d (int (array_get parts 2)))
(set n #1)

(set added 0)
(while (lt added n)
  (set d (add d 1))
  (if (gt d (dim y m))
    (set d 1)
    (set m (add m 1))
    (if (gt m 12) (set m 1) (set y (add y 1)) (else)))
  (set h (dow y m d))
  (if (and (ne h 0) (ne h 1))
    (set added (add added 1))))

(set ms (str m))
(if (lt m 10) (set ms (string_concat "0" ms)))
(set ds (str d))
(if (lt d 10) (set ds (string_concat "0" ds)))
(println (join [y ms ds] "-"))

Build a small map and print key=value entries sorted alphabetically by key, space-separated.

(let m {})
(set m (map_set m "a" 1))
(set m (map_set m "b" 2))
(set m (map_set m "c" 3))
(println (join (sort (map_kv m (\(k v) (fmt "{}={}" k v)))) " "))
